html:
[207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 挑一未字之舟女 - Literal phrase meaning 'choose an unmarried boat girl.' '舟女' refers to a woman who works on a boat, possibly as a singer or entertainer.
  • 与期婚 - Means 'to agree on marriage,' indicating a formal arrangement for marriage.
  • 冥中遂夺一举 - Ambiguous phrase. '冥中' can mean 'in the afterlife' or 'in the unseen realm,' while '夺一举' suggests that something was taken away or prevented, possibly referring to a significant event or opportunity.
  • 老脸撒泼 - Colloquial expression meaning 'to behave shamelessly' or 'to act in a way that disregards social norms.'
  • 强夺人妇 - Means 'to forcibly take someone else's wife,' indicating a violent or coercive act.
  • 功名反无梗 - Means 'success and reputation were not obstructed,' suggesting that despite the morally questionable actions, the person achieved success.
  • 何耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'why is this so?'
  • 岂此女合为夫人，特令丑始而令终与 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Is it that this woman was destined to be a lady, but fate made her life start ugly and end ugly?'
  • 天道亦僭赏矣 - Means 'Heaven's way also overstepped in its rewards,' suggesting that the outcome was unfair or unjust.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姚月华 - Name of a woman, likely a historical or literary figure.
  • 杨达 - Name of a man, likely a historical or literary figure.
  • 昭君怨 - Title of a poem, possibly referring to the famous Chinese poet Wang Zhaojun.
  • 款中散 - Term that is not clear from context, but it might refer to a type of drink or a ritual.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐卿 - Name of a man.
  • 莫生 - Name of a man.
  • 姚月华 - Name of a woman.
  • 杨达 - Name of a man.
  • 昭君怨 - Title of a poem.
  • 款中散 - Term that is not clear from context.

[208]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一日 - Simple temporal marker meaning 'one day' or 'a certain day.'
  • 飲於姚氏 - Means 'drinking at the Yao family,' indicating a social gathering.
  • 月华私命侍儿送合欢竹钿枕、温凉草文席 - Complex phrase describing a private command by the moon spirit to send a servant with a bamboo pillow and a grass mat, both items from the moon spirit's chamber.
  • 皆其香阁中物也 - Means 'all items from her fragrant chamber,' emphasizing the luxurious and intimate nature of the items.
  • 达虽心荡，亦无可奈何，怅然而归 - Means 'though his heart was stirred, he could do nothing, and returned home with a sense of loss.'
  • 次日，达奏笺送不律隃糜致谢 - Means 'the next day, he sent a note with a pen and ink to express his gratitude.'
  • 不律隃糜 - Refers to a type of pen and ink, with '不律' being a type of pen and '隃糜' being a type of ink.
  • 月华巧于丹青，然以自娱，人不可得而见 - Means 'the moon spirit is skilled in painting, but does so for her own amusement, and no one can see it.'
  • 是日，适画《芙蓉四鸟图》成，遂以答赠 - Means 'that day, she just finished painting a picture of a lotus with four birds and sent it as a gift.'
  • 达见其约略浓淡，生态逼真，爱玩不释 - Means 'he found the painting's subtle shades and lifelike portrayal so captivating that he couldn't put it down.'
  • 觅银光纸裁书谢之 - Means 'he found silver paper and wrote a thank-you note.'
  • 洒海刺二尺赠达曰 - Means 'she gave him a two-foot-long sword from the sea and said...' with '洒海刺' being a sword from the sea, often associated with magical or supernatural properties.
  • 为郎作履，凡履霜雪，则应履而解。乃西蕃物也 - Means 'for the young man to wear, when stepping on frost or snow, it should melt. It is a Western barbarian's treasure.'
  • 又贻诗曰 - Means 'she also gave him a poem saying...' with '西蕃' referring to Western barbarians, often used to describe people from the western regions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姚氏 - Refers to the Yao family, likely a noble or influential family.
  • 月华 - The moon spirit, a supernatural being often associated with beauty and grace.
  • 合欢竹钿枕 - A bamboo pillow decorated with jade, symbolizing harmony and luxury.
  • 温凉草文席 - A grass mat with warm and cool patterns, indicating a high-quality and comfortable bedding.
  • 达 - A common name, likely referring to a male character in the story.
  • 丹青 - Refers to painting, often associated with fine arts.
  • 芙蓉四鸟图 - A painting of a lotus with four birds, symbolizing beauty and harmony.
  • 银光纸 - Silver paper, likely a high-quality writing material.
  • 西蕃 - Refers to Western barbarians, often used to describe people from the western regions.

Proper Nouns

  • 姚氏 - The Yao family.
  • 月华 - The moon spirit.
  • 达 - A character in the story.
  • 不律隃糜 - A type of pen and ink.
  • 芙蓉四鸟图 - A painting of a lotus with four birds.
  • 银光纸 - Silver paper.
  • 洒海刺 - A sword from the sea.
  • 西蕃 - Western barbarians.

[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 金刀剪紫绒 - Metaphorical expression where '金刀' (golden knife) and '紫绒' (purple velvet) symbolize fine craftsmanship and luxury.
  • 与郎作轻履 - Literal phrase meaning 'to make light shoes for the young man,' but it carries a deeper meaning of creating something delicate and precious for a loved one.
  • 愿化双仙凫 - Metaphorical expression where '双仙凫' (two fairy mandarin ducks) symbolize a desire for eternal companionship and harmony.
  • 飞来入闺里 - Poetic phrase meaning 'fly into the inner chamber,' symbolizing the wish for the beloved to be always present.
  • 心醉若狂 - Vivid expression meaning 'to be intoxicated and mad with joy,' describing an intense emotional state.
  • 大会 - Term referring to a significant meeting or gathering.
  • 小会 - Term referring to a minor or casual meeting.
  • 鹣鹣会 - Metaphorical term where '鹣鹣' (mandarin ducks) symbolize a harmonious and intimate relationship.
  • 白鹢会 - Metaphorical term where '白鹢' (white swans) symbolize purity and grace in a relationship.
  • 减食 - Literal phrase meaning 'to reduce food intake,' but it can also symbolize a decrease in vitality or happiness.
  • 效徐淑体 - Reference to the literary style of Xu Shu, a famous female writer, indicating a high level of literary skill.
  • 萱草兮徒树 - Metaphorical expression where '萱草' (daylily) symbolizes forgetfulness or relief from sorrow, but '徒树' (in vain) suggests that this relief is unattainable.
  • 幸逢兮君子 - Phrase meaning 'fortunate to meet a gentleman,' expressing gratitude for the beloved's presence.
  • 许结兮殷勤 - Phrase meaning 'to promise a deep and sincere bond,' indicating a strong commitment.
  • 分香兮剪发 - Metaphorical expression where '分香' (dividing fragrance) and '剪发' (cutting hair) symbolize intimate and personal acts of devotion.
  • 赠玉兮共珍 - Phrase meaning 'to give jade and share its value,' symbolizing the exchange of precious gifts and mutual appreciation.
  • 指天兮结誓 - Phrase meaning 'to swear by heaven,' indicating a solemn and binding promise.
  • 愿为兮一身 - Phrase meaning 'to wish to be one body,' symbolizing a desire for unity and oneness.
  • 所遭兮多舛 - Phrase meaning 'to encounter many misfortunes,' indicating a series of unfortunate events.
  • 玉体兮难亲 - Phrase meaning 'the precious body is hard to be close to,' suggesting a physical or emotional distance.
  • 损餐兮减寝 - Phrase meaning 'to reduce meals and sleep,' symbolizing a decline in health and well-being due to sorrow.
  • 带缓兮罗裙 - Phrase meaning 'the belt of the silk skirt loosens,' symbolizing a loss of vitality or energy.
  • 菱鉴兮慵启 - Phrase meaning 'the lotus mirror is lazily opened,' symbolizing a lack of interest in grooming or self-care.
  • 博炉兮焉薰 - Phrase meaning 'the incense burner is not used,' symbolizing a loss of pleasure or comfort.
  • 整袜兮欲举 - Phrase meaning 'to prepare to put on socks,' symbolizing the intention to move forward or take action.
  • 塞路兮荆榛 - Phrase meaning 'the path is blocked by thorns,' symbolizing obstacles or difficulties.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金刀 - Term often used in classical literature to symbolize fine craftsmanship or luxury.
  • 紫绒 - Purple velvet, a luxurious material often associated with wealth and nobility.
  • 轻履 - Light shoes, symbolizing delicacy and grace.
  • 仙凫 - Fairy mandarin ducks, symbols of eternal companionship and harmony.
  • 闺里 - The inner chamber of a woman's home, symbolizing privacy and intimacy.
  • 大会 - A significant meeting or gathering, often with formal or ceremonial importance.
  • 小会 - A minor or casual meeting, often more intimate and informal.
  • 鹣鹣会 - A metaphorical term for a harmonious and intimate relationship.
  • 白鹢会 - A metaphorical term for a pure and graceful relationship.
  • 萱草 - Daylily, a plant often used in classical poetry to symbolize forgetfulness or relief from sorrow.
  • 徐淑体 - The literary style of Xu Shu, a famous female writer known for her elegant and refined prose.
  • 玉体 - Precious body, a term of endearment often used to describe a beloved.
  • 分香 - Dividing fragrance, a metaphor for intimate and personal acts of devotion.
  • 剪发 - Cutting hair, another metaphor for intimate and personal acts of devotion.
  • 赠玉 - Giving jade, a symbol of precious gifts and mutual appreciation.
  • 指天 - Swearing by heaven, a solemn and binding promise.
  • 所遭 - Encountered, a term indicating the experience of events.
  • 多舛 - Many misfortunes, a term indicating a series of unfortunate events.
  • 玉体 - Precious body, a term of endearment often used to describe a beloved.
  • 损餐 - Reducing meals, a symbol of a decline in health and well-being.
  • 减寝 - Reducing sleep, another symbol of a decline in health and well-being.
  • 带缓 - Loosening the belt, a symbol of a loss of vitality or energy.
  • 罗裙 - Silk skirt, a symbol of elegance and grace.
  • 菱鉴 - Lotus mirror, a symbol of self-reflection and grooming.
  • 博炉 - Incense burner, a symbol of comfort and pleasure.
  • 整袜 - Preparing to put on socks, a symbol of intention to move forward.
  • 塞路 - Blocking the path, a symbol of obstacles or difficulties.
  • 荆榛 - Thorns, a symbol of obstacles or difficulties.

[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 欲語，鞈匝兮頑嚣 - Poetic expression where '欲語' means 'wanting to speak,' '鞈匝' means 'surrounded' or 'encircled,' and '頑嚣' means 'stupid noise' or 'foolish clamor.' Together, it suggests a desire to speak but being surrounded by foolish noise.
  • 煩冤兮憑胸 - '煩冤' means 'anxiety' or 'trouble,' and '憑胸' means 'pressing on the chest,' indicating deep emotional distress.
  • 何時兮可論 - Rhetorical question meaning 'when can this be discussed?' expressing frustration and despair.
  • 願君兮見察 - '願君' means 'I wish for you,' and '見察' means 'to see and understand,' indicating a plea for understanding.
  • 安死兮何瞋 - '安死' means 'peaceful death,' and '何瞋' means 'why be angry,' expressing a desire for a peaceful death and a plea for not being angry.
  • 呜咽不胜 - '呜咽' means 'sobbing,' and '不胜' means 'unable to bear,' indicating intense emotional distress.
  • 曾整装向江右踪迹之 - '曾' means 'once,' '整装' means 'to prepare,' '向' means 'to go to,' '江右' means 'west of the river,' and '踪迹之' means 'to trace,' indicating an attempt to find someone.
  • 每为友道及，辄呜呜泣下云 - '每' means 'every time,' '为友道及' means 'when told by friends,' '辄' means 'always,' '呜呜泣下' means 'sobbing,' and '云' means 'said,' indicating a recurring emotional reaction.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 弱冠 - A traditional Chinese term for a young man around 20 years old, marking the age of coming of age.
  • 市有孙翁造白扇 - Describes a market stall where Mr. Sun makes white fans, reflecting the common practice of artisans selling their goods in markets.
  • 日往买扇 - Reflects the daily routine of buying goods, a common activity in traditional Chinese society.
  • 冀时睹芳容耳 - Expresses a common romantic desire to see a woman's beauty.
  • 遗以香囊、汗巾并银簪一枝 - Describes the exchange of gifts, a common practice in romantic relationships.
  • 约某夕会于后门 - Reflects the secrecy and discretion often associated with romantic meetings.
  • 数日以待 - Reflects the anticipation and waiting that often accompany romantic pursuits.
  • 夜半女出 - Reflects the secrecy and discretion often associated with romantic meetings.
  • 不暇自顾 - Reflects the intensity and urgency of the moment.
  • 勉强交欢 - Reflects the physical and emotional intensity of the encounter.
  • 扶生墙下 - Reflects the care and concern for the man's well-being.
  • 掩门而入 - Reflects the secrecy and discretion often associated with romantic meetings.
  • 邻人见生死 - Reflects the community's awareness and involvement in personal matters.
  • 驰报林翁 - Reflects the urgency and importance of the news.
  • 罔知其由 - Reflects the confusion and lack of understanding often experienced in such situations.
  • 因葬之 - Reflects the traditional practice of burying the dead.
  • 知不可讳 - Reflects the inevitability of the truth and the need to face it.
  • 以实告 - Reflects the importance of honesty and transparency.
  • 尔富而无子 - Reflects the traditional emphasis on having children, especially sons.
  • 止此女 - Reflects the traditional emphasis on family and lineage.
  • 今幸孕 - Reflects the joy and hope associated with pregnancy.
  • 倘为一子 - Reflects the traditional emphasis on having sons.
  • 亦吾嫡甥也 - Reflects the traditional emphasis on family and lineage.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 欲語，鞈匝兮頑嚣 - This poetic expression must be read as a single unit, describing the man's desire to speak but being surrounded by foolish noise.
  • 煩冤兮憑胸，何時兮可論 - This rhetorical question must be read as a single unit, expressing deep emotional distress and frustration.
  • 願君兮見察，安死兮何瞋 - This plea must be read as a single unit, expressing a desire for understanding and a plea for not being angry.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 翁然之 - 翁然 means 'slowly' or 'gradually,' and 之 is a particle indicating the action of the subject. Together, it suggests a gradual acceptance or compliance.
  • 惧人知 - 惧 means 'fear' or 'dread,' and 人知 means 'people knowing.' This phrase conveys the fear of being discovered.
  • 弃业 - 弃 means 'abandon,' and 业 means 'occupation' or 'business.' Together, it means 'to abandon one's job.'
  • 移居他所 - 移居 means 'to move residence,' and 他所 means 'another place.' This phrase indicates moving to a different location.
  • 未几 - 未几 means 'not long after' or 'shortly.'
  • 女生子长数岁矣 - 女 means 'woman,' 生子 means 'give birth to a son,' 长数岁 means 'grow several years old,' and 矣 is a particle indicating completion. This phrase describes the woman giving birth to a son who has grown to be several years old.
  • 偶适市 - 偶 means 'accidentally' or 'by chance,' 适 means 'to go to,' and 市 means 'market.' This phrase means 'accidentally going to the market.'
  • 过林翁门 - 过 means 'to pass by,' 林翁 means 'Mr. Lin,' and 门 means 'gate' or 'door.' This phrase means 'passing by Mr. Lin's gate.'
  • 林夫妇见之曰 - 林夫妇 means 'Mr. and Mrs. Lin,' 见 means 'see,' 之 is a particle indicating the object, and 曰 means 'said.' This phrase means 'Mr. and Mrs. Lin saw him and said.'
  • 此何人子？酷似亡儿 - 此 means 'this,' 何人子 means 'whose son,' 酷似 means 'very similar to,' and 亡儿 means 'deceased son.' This phrase means 'Whose son is this? He looks very much like our deceased son.'
  • 相与挥泣 - 相与 means 'together,' 挥泣 means 'to shed tears.' This phrase means 'they shed tears together.'
  • 遂携儿至家，与之果 - 遂 means 'then,' 携 means 'carry,' 儿 means 'son,' 至家 means 'to the house,' 与之 means 'give to him,' and 果 means 'fruit.' This phrase means 'they then carried the boy to their house and gave him fruit.'
  • 儿归告母，母告其父 - 儿 means 'son,' 归 means 'return,' 告 means 'tell,' 母 means 'mother,' and 其父 means 'his father.' This phrase means 'the boy returned home and told his mother, who then told his father.'
  • 使访其亡子姓名，且有遗物否 - 使 means 'send,' 访 means 'inquire,' 其亡子 means 'their deceased son,' 姓名 means 'name,' 且 means 'and,' 有 means 'have,' 遗物 means 'leftover items,' and 否 means 'whether.' This phrase means 'they sent someone to inquire about the name of their deceased son and whether there were any leftover items.'
  • 孙翁携儿往，林翁延之 - 孙翁 means 'Mr. Sun,' 携 means 'carry,' 儿 means 'boy,' 往 means 'go,' 林翁 means 'Mr. Lin,' 延 means 'invite,' and 之 is a particle indicating the object. This phrase means 'Mr. Sun carried the boy to Mr. Lin's house, and Mr. Lin invited them in.'
  • 各言子之姓名、年貌 - 各 means 'each,' 言 means 'say,' 子 means 'son,' 之 is a particle indicating the object, 姓名 means 'name,' 年貌 means 'age and appearance.' This phrase means 'they each said the name, age, and appearance of their son.'
  • 其时死于孙翁后门 - 其时 means 'at that time,' 死 means 'die,' 于 means 'in,' 孙翁后门 means 'behind Mr. Sun's house.' This phrase means 'at that time, he died behind Mr. Sun's house.'
  • 孙问林子所遗物 - 孙 means 'Mr. Sun,' 问 means 'ask,' 林子 means 'Mr. Lin's son,' 所 means 'the,' and 遗物 means 'leftover items.' This phrase means 'Mr. Sun asked about the leftover items of Mr. Lin's son.'
  • 林翁曰：“吾儿有书馆，自殁至今不忍开。” - 林翁 means 'Mr. Lin,' 曰 means 'said,' 吾儿 means 'my son,' 有 means 'has,' 书馆 means 'study room,' 自 means 'since,' 殁 means 'died,' 至今 means 'until now,' and 不忍开 means 'cannot bear to open.' This phrase means 'Mr. Lin said, 'My son had a study room, and I cannot bear to open it since his death until now.'
  • 因至馆启锁 - 因 means 'then,' 至 means 'go to,' 馆 means 'study room,' and 启锁 means 'unlock.' This phrase means 'they then went to the study room and unlocked it.'
  • 尘坌堆积 - 尘坌 means 'dust and dirt,' and 堆积 means 'accumulated.' This phrase means 'dust and dirt had accumulated.'
  • 卧房一箱中，有白扇、汗巾及银簪 - 卧房 means 'bedroom,' 一箱中 means 'in a box,' 有 means 'have,' 白扇 means 'white fan,' 汗巾 means 'handkerchief,' 及 means 'and,' and 银簪 means 'silver hairpin.' This phrase means 'in the bedroom box, there were a white fan, a handkerchief, and a silver hairpin.'
  • 孙念扇皆己家物，香囊又类其女手制 - 孙 means 'Mr. Sun,' 念 means 'think,' 扇 means 'fans,' 皆 means 'all,' 己家物 means 'items from his own family,' 香囊 means 'perfume sachet,' 又 means 'also,' 类 means 'resemble,' 其女 means 'his daughter,' and 手制 means 'made by hand.' This phrase means 'Mr. Sun thought that the fans were all items from his own family, and the perfume sachet also resembled the one his daughter made by hand.'
  • 遂并求三物，归以示女 - 遂 means 'then,' 并 means 'and,' 求 means 'ask for,' 三物 means 'three items,' 归 means 'return,' 以 means 'to,' 示 means 'show,' and 女 means 'daughter.' This phrase means 'he then asked for the three items, returned home, and showed them to his daughter.'
  • 女泣曰：“此皆前赠林者，此子果林子也。” - 女 means 'daughter,' 泣 means 'cry,' 曰 means 'said,' 此 means 'these,' 皆 means 'all,' 前 means 'before,' 赠 means 'give,' 林 means 'Mr. Lin,' 者 means 'things,' 果 means 'indeed,' and 林子 means 'Mr. Lin's son.' This phrase means 'the daughter cried and said, 'These are all things I gave to Mr. Lin before. This boy is indeed Mr. Lin's son.'
  • 孙翁走告林，林大喜，以为自天降 - 孙翁 means 'Mr. Sun,' 走 means 'run,' 告 means 'tell,' 林 means 'Mr. Lin,' 大喜 means 'very happy,' 以为 means 'think,' and 自天降 means 'from heaven.' This phrase means 'Mr. Sun ran to tell Mr. Lin, who was very happy and thought it was a gift from heaven.'
  • 乃二姓合居，共教其子，登科甲，为显宦 - 乃 means 'then,' 二姓 means 'two families,' 合居 means 'live together,' 共 means 'together,' 教 means 'teach,' 其子 means 'their son,' 登科甲 means 'pass the imperial examinations,' 为 means 'become,' and 显宦 means 'high-ranking official.' This phrase means 'then the two families lived together, taught their son, and he passed the imperial examinations and became a high-ranking official.'

[212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 淳熙中 - Refers to the period during the reign of Emperor Xiaozong of Song (1174–1189).
  • 美姿容 - Describes someone with a beautiful appearance.
  • 赋性温茂 - Means 'by nature warm and generous,' describing a person's temperament.
  • 尤善丝竹 - Indicates a particular skill in playing string and wind instruments.
  • 时以三郎称之 - Suggests that he was commonly referred to as 'Third Brother' or 'Third Young Master.'
  • 上元夜 - Refers to the Lantern Festival, which falls on the 15th day of the first lunar month.
  • 击筑飞觞 - A combination of playing a zither (筑) and passing wine cups (飞觞), indicating a lively and festive atmosphere.
  • 呼卢博胜 - Refers to a gambling game called 'hu lu,' where players bet on dice rolls.
  • 长夜之欢 - Means 'a night of joy' or 'a long night of revelry.'
  • 漏尽铜龙 - Refers to the sound of a water clock (铜龙) indicating that the night has passed.
  • 皓月满轮 - Describes a full, bright moon.
  • 浮光耀采 - Means 'floating light and brilliant colors,' describing the moonlight.
  • 欣然 - Indicates a joyful and delighted state of mind.
  • 见此景而归枕席 - Means 'to return to bed upon seeing this scene.'
  • 孰若各事所能 - Means 'why not each do what they can.'
  • 共乐清光之下 - Means 'to enjoy the clear moonlight together.'
  • 善 - Indicates agreement or approval.
  • 紫玉箫 - Refers to a type of flute made of purple jade.
  • 声入云表 - Means 'the sound reaches the clouds,' describing a very beautiful and resonant sound.
  • 陈太常子 - Refers to the son of Chen Taichang, a high-ranking official.
  • 灯筵方散 - Means 'the lantern banquet has just ended.'
  • 步月于庭 - Means 'to stroll in the courtyard under the moonlight.'
  • 凝睇者久之 - Means 'to gaze fixedly for a long time.'
  • 低讽一绝 - Means 'to recite a short poem softly.'
  • 夜色沉沉月满庭 - Describes a scene of deep night with a full moon illuminating the courtyard.
  • 是谁吹彻绕云声 - Means 'who is playing the flute that its sound echoes among the clouds?'
  • 呜呜只管翻新调 - Means 'the flute keeps playing new tunes.'
  • 那顾愁人泪眼倾 - Means 'who cares about the sorrowful person whose tears flow?'
  • 遂怏怏而入 - Describes the daughter's mood after the event.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 淳熙 - The era name of Emperor Xiaozong of Song, indicating the historical period.
  • 上元夜 - The Lantern Festival, a significant traditional Chinese festival.
  • 击筑 - An ancient musical instrument, often associated with historical figures like Jing Ke.
  • 呼卢博胜 - A traditional Chinese gambling game, reflecting the cultural practice of entertainment during festivals.
  • 漏尽铜龙 - A reference to the ancient Chinese water clock, symbolizing the passage of time.
  • 皓月满轮 - A poetic description of the full moon, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 紫玉箫 - A type of flute, often associated with elegance and refinement in classical Chinese poetry.
  • 陈太常子 - Chen Taichang was a high-ranking official during the Song Dynasty, indicating the social status of the family.
  • 灯筵 - A traditional Chinese banquet held during the Lantern Festival, often involving music, dance, and poetry.

Proper Nouns

  • 阮华 - The main character's name.
  • 陈太常子 - The son of Chen Taichang, a high-ranking official.
  • 玉兰 - The name of the daughter of Chen Taichang.
  • 三郎 - A common nickname for the third son or a respectful title for a young man.

[213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一夕 - Refers to 'one night' or 'one evening.'

  • 師何來若此早 - Why did you come so early? A direct question expressing surprise.

  • 翌日告成 - It will be completed tomorrow, indicating a future event.
  • 愿夫人与小姐随喜一观 - I hope you and your daughter will come to admire it, a polite invitation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 清夜凄凄 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'on a clear, chilly night,' evoking a sense of solitude and melancholy.
  • 挑灯欲自写愁肠 - Literally 'picking up the lamp to write out one's sorrows,' it means to sit up late at night writing about one's worries or sorrows.
  • 相思未诉魂先断 - Poetic expression meaning 'before the longing can be expressed, the spirit is already broken,' emphasizing the intensity of unspoken longing.
  • 一字书成泪万行 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'one character written brings forth countless tears,' highlighting the emotional weight of writing about sorrow.
  • 玉漏催残到枕边 - Refers to the sound of the ancient water clock (玉漏) reminding the person of the passage of time until bedtime.
  • 孤帏此际转凄然 - Means 'in this moment, alone under the canopy, the feeling of desolation grows stronger,' describing a deepening sense of loneliness.
  • 不知寂寞嫌更永 - Rhetorical question meaning 'does not know if the loneliness makes the night seem longer,' expressing the perception that loneliness exaggerates the duration of the night.
  • 却恨更筹有万千 - Means 'but resents the countless hours of the night,' indicating frustration with the length of the night.
  • 朝来独倚绮窗前 - Literally 'in the morning, alone leaning against the beautiful window,' it describes a solitary act of contemplation.
  • 试探何时了此缘 - Means 'testing when this bond will end,' suggesting an attempt to understand the duration of a relationship or connection.
  • 每日殷勤偷问卜 - Means 'every day, diligently secretly seeking divination,' indicating a daily practice of seeking guidance through fortune-telling.
  • 不知掷破几多钱 - Rhetorical question meaning 'does not know how much money has been spent,' implying the cost of these practices.
  • 闭于小轩邃室 - Means 'confined in a small, secluded room,' describing a private and isolated space.
  • 联翩而至 - Means 'arriving in succession,' indicating a continuous stream of arrivals.
  • 旁设一门，随手可启 - Means 'a door set on the side, which can be opened at will,' indicating a convenient entrance or exit.
  • 华自床后忽来 - Means 'Hua suddenly appears from behind the bed,' describing an unexpected appearance.

  • 绮窗 - Refers to a beautifully decorated window, often used in classical poetry to evoke a sense of elegance and beauty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 行云一梦断巫阳 - Poetic expression meaning 'a dream of clouds dispersing in Wuchang,' where Wuchang is a place name, possibly referring to a legendary or mythological location. The phrase conveys a sense of lost dreams or fading memories.
  • 懒向台前理旧妆 - Literally means 'lazy to go before the mirror to fix the old makeup,' symbolizing neglect or lack of interest in one's appearance or past.
  • 憔悴不胜羞对镜 - Means 'too emaciated to bear looking at oneself in the mirror,' indicating deep emotional distress or self-reproach.
  • 为谁梳洗整容光 - Literally 'for whom to comb, wash, and arrange one's appearance,' expressing a sense of purposelessness or loss of motivation.
  • 几向花间想旧踪 - Means 'how many times have I thought of my old traces in the flowers,' suggesting nostalgia or longing for the past.
  • 徘徊花下有谁同 - Literally 'wandering under the flowers, who is with me?' conveys loneliness or the absence of companionship.
  • 可怜多少相思泪 - Means 'pitiful are the countless tears of longing,' expressing deep emotional pain or sorrow.
  • 染得花枝片片红 - Literally 'staining the branches of flowers red,' metaphorically describes tears or emotions coloring the surroundings.
  • 一自风波起楚台 - Means 'since the storm arose on the Chu platform,' where 'Chu platform' could refer to a specific location or symbolize a time of turmoil.
  • 深闺冷落已堪哀 - Literally 'the inner chambers are lonely and cold, already worthy of pity,' indicating isolation or neglect.
  • 余烟空自消金鸭 - Means 'the remaining smoke from the golden duck incense burner fades away in vain,' symbolizing the passage of time or the fading of memories.
  • 那得芳心化作灰 - Literally 'how can the fragrant heart turn to ashes?' expresses despair or the loss of hope.
  • 云和独抱不成眠 - Means 'the cloud and harmony embrace each other, unable to sleep,' possibly symbolizing unrequited love or emotional turmoil.
  • 移向庭前月满天 - Literally 'moving to the courtyard where the moon fills the sky,' evokes a sense of solitude or reflection under the moonlight.
  • 别怨一声双泪落 - Means 'a single word of parting causes tears to fall,' expressing sorrow or regret.
  • 可怜点点湿朱弦 - Literally 'pitiful are the tears that wet the red strings of the zither,' symbolizing emotional pain or the impact of tears on one's surroundings.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 巫阳 - A place name, possibly referring to a legendary or mythological location. In Chinese literature, 'Wuchang' often symbolizes a place of dreams or memories.
  • 楚台 - Refers to a specific location or symbolizes a time of turmoil or change. 'Chu' is an ancient Chinese state, and 'platform' could refer to a physical structure or a metaphorical place of significance.
  • 金鸭 - Refers to a golden duck incense burner, a common object in ancient Chinese households used for burning incense.
  • 朱弦 - Refers to the red strings of a zither, a traditional Chinese musical instrument. The zither is often associated with elegance and refinement.
  • 阮氏 - Refers to the Ruan family, a specific family name. In Chinese culture, family names are important and often carry historical or social significance.
  • 州牧 - Refers to a high-ranking official in ancient China, typically the governor of a state or region. This title indicates a position of significant authority and responsibility.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 伪吴有国 - Compound phrase meaning 'the pseudo-Wu state existed,' where '伪' (pseudonymous) implies a state that is not fully recognized or is a pretender to the throne.
  • 中乐桥李卖线之女美 - Describes a beautiful woman from a family of silk sellers in the Middle Joy Bridge area. '卖线' (mǎixiàn) refers to selling silk thread.
  • 司徒李伯昇之子悦之 - '悦之' (yuè zhī) means 'was pleased with her' or 'was attracted to her.'
  • 日倚其门 - Literally 'daily leaning on her door,' this idiom suggests frequent visits or lingering near her home.
  • 一尼为定计，诱致之室 - '一尼' (yī ní) means 'one nun,' and '为定计' (wéi dìng jì) means 'made a plan.' '诱致之室' (yòu zhì zhī shì) means 'lured her into the room.'
  • 李子喜极，一交接即死 - '喜极' (xǐ jí) means 'extremely happy,' and '一交接即死' (yī jiāojiē jí sǐ) means 'died immediately after having intercourse.'
  • 尼瘗其尸榻下 - '瘗' (yì) means 'bury,' and '榻下' (tà xià) means 'under the bed.'
  • 置其所带大帽于床顶 - '置' (zhì) means 'place,' '其带大帽' (qí dài dà mào) means 'the large hat she was wearing,' and '床顶' (chuáng dǐng) means 'on the bed.'
  • 未几屋漏 - '未几' (wèi jǐ) means 'not long after,' and '屋漏' (wū lòu) means 'roof leak.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Several months later, Didi's husband returned. The man, a small person, had secretly planned to win over Didi and could not abandon the heavy bribe. He waited until her husband was sitting with guests, then sent a servant to report, saying, 'A certain official once sold pearls worth two thousand strings of cash in my house, but long had not received the value, and is now suing me in court.' The husband was startled and stared, then went to question. Didi was at a loss for words and said, 'Yes.' The husband demanded the return of the pearls. The man got the pearls and again sent a nun to thank Didi, saying, 'How did I get these? I borrowed from relatives to move you!' Though Didi was very angry, she could not ultimately forget him. When the husband left, she would call him and have relations. After a year, the husband discovered and kept a strict watch. Didi died from illness.

  • 花落花开自有时，总赖东君主 - A metaphorical expression indicating that natural cycles have their own timing, and ultimately, they are governed by a higher power (often referring to the East Wind, a symbol of spring and renewal). It can be translated as 'Flowers bloom and fall at their own time, all depending on the East Wind.'
  • 去也终须去，住也如何住 - A rhetorical question expressing the inevitability of change and the futility of resistance. It can be translated as 'Leaving is inevitable, and staying is impossible.'
  • 若得山花插满头，莫问奴归处 - A poetic expression of contentment and resignation, suggesting that if one can find happiness in simple pleasures, they should not worry about their ultimate destination. It can be translated as 'If I can fill my head with mountain flowers, do not ask where I will go.'

  • 蛛忙鹊懒，耕慵织倦 - Refers to the legend of the spider and the magpie, where the spider weaves a web to help the magpie bridge the Milky Way. The phrase suggests that both the spider and the magpie are tired and lazy.

  • 昨见所话 - Means 'what we discussed yesterday.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 侯門一入深如海 - Metaphor comparing entering a noble's house to diving into the ocean, suggesting the profound and often overwhelming nature of such an experience.
  • 從此蕭郎是路人 - '蕭郎' is a term of endearment for a lover, and '是路人' means 'becomes a stranger,' indicating a sudden estrangement or loss of affection.
  • 或有嫉郊者 - '嫉郊' means to envy or resent someone, in this case, the poet Jiao Ran.
  • 寫詩於座 - Means 'wrote a poem at the gathering,' indicating a social event where poetry was composed.
  • 握郊手曰 - Means 'took Jiao's hand and said,' showing a gesture of reassurance or affection.
  • 四百千小哉 - '四百千' is an old monetary unit, and '小哉' means 'trivial' or 'insignificant,' suggesting that the amount is not worth worrying about.
  • 何惜一書，不早相示 - Means 'why not show it to me earlier,' expressing regret that the poem was not shown sooner.
  • 遂命婢同歸 - Means 'then ordered the maid to accompany them home,' indicating a gesture of hospitality or care.
  • 至帏幌奁匣，悉為增饰之 - '帏幌奁匣' refers to various household items, and '悉為增饰之' means 'all were adorned and enhanced,' showing attention to detail and care.
  • 崔生因此小阜 - '小阜' means 'slightly improved' or 'prospered,' indicating a positive change in the person's circumstances.

  • 遽命召焉 - Means 'immediately ordered to summon him,' showing urgency or importance.
  • 逾月而至 - Means 'arrived after a month,' indicating a delay.
  • 令唱歌，歌乃戎使君送妓之什也 - '令唱歌' means 'ordered to sing,' and '歌乃戎使君送妓之什也' means 'the song was one that Rong made to send his concubine away,' indicating a specific context for the song.
  • 丈夫不能立功业，为异代之所称 - Means 'a man cannot establish achievements that will be praised by future generations,' expressing a sense of regret or disappointment.

  • 明旦婴起，见妻杀死，愕然，欲出自白 - 明旦 means the next morning. 愕然 means to be astonished or shocked. 欲出自白 means to want to come forward and confess.
  • 婴邻以为真婴杀，留缚之 - 婴邻 refers to the neighbors of the family. 以为真婴杀 means they believe the husband actually killed his wife. 留缚之 means to detain and bind him.
  • 趋告妻党 - 趋告 means to rush and inform. 妻党 refers to the wife's relatives.

  • 官收系杀人罪，莫有辨者，强伏其辜 - 官收系 means the officials detained him. 杀人罪 means the crime of murder. 莫有辨者 means no one could defend him. 强伏其辜 means he was forced to admit guilt.
  • 司法官与小吏持朴者数十人，将婴就市 - 司法官 means the judicial officials. 小吏持朴者 means minor officials carrying sticks (for beating). 数十人 means several dozen people. 将婴就市 means to lead the husband to the market (for execution).
  • 看者千余人 - 看者 means spectators. 千余人 means more than a thousand people.
  • 有一人排众而来，呼曰 - 排众而来 means to push through the crowd and come forward. 呼曰 means to call out and say.
  • 且无令不辜死 - 且无令 means let there be no order. 不辜死 means to die without guilt.
  • 吾窃其妻，而又杀之，当系我 - 窃其妻 means to steal his wife. 而又杀之 means and then kill her. 当系我 means I should be detained.
  • 吏执自言人，乃燕也 - 吏执 means the officials detained. 自言人 means the person who spoke up. 乃燕也 means it was Yan.
  • 与燕俱见耽，尽以状对 - 与燕俱见耽 means both were brought before the official. 尽以状对 means to fully state the facts.
  • 耽乃状闻，请归其印，以赎其死 - 耽乃状闻 means the official reported the facts. 请归其印 means requested to return his seal. 以赎其死 means to redeem his life.
  • 上谊之，下诏，凡滑城死罪者皆免 - 上谊之 means the emperor approved it. 下诏 means issued an edict. 凡滑城死罪者皆免 means all those sentenced to death in Huacheng were pardoned.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 成帝雖惑於微行，而存心撫民，無勞無怨 - Emperor Cheng, though entangled in secret outings, still had a heart to comfort the people, without causing labor or resentment.
  • 及劉向、谷永切諫，遂焚宵遊宮及飛行殿，罷宴逸之樂，庶幾從繩則正者矣 - When Liu Xiang and Gu Yong gave sharp advice, he burned down the night-wandering palace and the flying pavilion, abolished the pleasures of feasting and leisure, almost following the rope to be upright.
  • 吳處以椒華之房，貫細珠為簾幌 - Wu placed them in rooms adorned with pepper and flowers, and strung fine pearls to make curtains.
  • 吳王妖惑忘政 - King Fuchai was bewitched and forgot his duties as a ruler.
  • 今吳城蛇門內有朽株，尚為祠神女之處雲 - Now, inside the snake gate of Wu city, there is a decaying tree, which is still a place of worship for the goddess.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 成帝 - Refers to Emperor Cheng of Han, a historical figure.
  • 劉向、谷永 - Refers to Liu Xiang and Gu Yong, historical figures who were Confucian scholars and advisors.
  • 《西京雜記》 - A historical text that records various anecdotes and stories about the Western Han capital, Chang'an.
  • 吳王夫差 - Refers to King Fuchai of Wu, a historical figure known for his role in the Warring States period.
  • 越 - Refers to the state of Yue, a historical kingdom that was a rival to Wu.
  • 椒華之房 - Refers to a room adorned with pepper and flowers, symbolizing luxury and beauty.
  • 神女 - Refers to a goddess or divine woman, often associated with supernatural beauty and power.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '成帝雖惑於微行，而存心撫民，無勞無怨' is a concessive construction, meaning 'Although Emperor Cheng was entangled in secret outings, he still had a heart to comfort the people, without causing labor or resentment.'
  • The sentence '及劉向、谷永切諫，遂焚宵遊宮及飛行殿，罷宴逸之樂，庶幾從繩則正者矣' is a sequence of actions, meaning 'When Liu Xiang and Gu Yong gave sharp advice, he burned down the night-wandering palace and the flying pavilion, abolished the pleasures of feasting and leisure, almost following the rope to be upright.'
  • The sentence '吳處以椒華之房，貫細珠為簾幌' is a descriptive construction, meaning 'Wu placed them in rooms adorned with pepper and flowers, and strung fine pearls to make curtains.'
  • The sentence '吳王妖惑忘政' is a cause-and-effect construction, meaning 'King Fuchai was bewitched and forgot his duties as a ruler.'
  • The sentence '今吳城蛇門內有朽株，尚為祠神女之處雲' is a descriptive construction, meaning 'Now, inside the snake gate of Wu city, there is a decaying tree, which is still a place of worship for the goddess.'

  • 又筑土为台，基三十丈，列烛致于台下 - Describes a large platform being built with thirty zhang (a unit of measurement) of earth, and candles being placed at its base.

  • 又于大道之旁，一里量一铜表，高五尺，以志里数 - Describes bronze markers being placed every li (a unit of distance) along the road, each five chi (a unit of height) tall, to mark the distance.

  • 为铜表于道侧，是土上出金之义 - Explains that the bronze markers symbolize the emergence of gold from the earth.
  • 以烛致台下，则火在土下之义 - Indicates that the candles placed at the base of the platform symbolize fire beneath the earth.
  • 汉火德王，魏土德王，火伏而土兴也 - Explains that the Han Dynasty was ruled by the fire element, while the Wei Dynasty was ruled by the earth element, suggesting a transition from fire to earth.
  • 土上出金，是魏灭晋兴也 - Suggests that the emergence of gold from the earth symbolizes the fall of the Wei Dynasty and the rise of the Jin Dynasty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '灵芸未至京师十里，帝乘雕玉之辇，以望车徒之盛' describes the emperor waiting for Ling Yun to arrive, but the exact relationship between the two events is unclear.
  • The phrase '非云非雨，非朝非暮' is a playful response to the allusion, but its exact meaning is open to interpretation.
  • The sentence '入宫承宠爱' is elliptical, but it suggests that Ling Yun was taken into the palace and received imperial favor.
  • The sentence '夜来妙于针功' is elliptical, but it suggests that Yela (夜来) excelled in needlework, even in dimly lit spaces.
  • The sentence '所居之室，名为“思香媚寝”' is elliptical, but it suggests that the bedroom was named for its fragrant allure.

Proper Nouns

  • 灵芸 - The name of the female protagonist.
  • 吴主亮 - The name of the ruler of Wu.
  • 夜来 - The new name given to Ling Yun.
  • 百濯香 - The name of a special perfume.
  • 思香媚寝 - The name of a luxurious bedroom.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 《烟花记》云 - Citation formula meaning 'The *Flower and Fire Record* states.'
  • 命宫人潘芳作琉璃屏风 - Ordered the palace woman Pan Fang to make a glass screen. '琉璃' can be translated as 'glass' or 'lapis lazuli,' but in this context, 'glass' is more appropriate.
  • 镂祥物一百三十种 - Inlaid with auspicious objects of one hundred and thirty kinds. '祥物' refers to auspicious or symbolic objects.
  • 各有生气 - 'Each has vitality,' meaning that each object appears lifelike.
  • 远视若真 - 'When viewed from a distance, they appear real.'

  • 顿之飞去 - 'When it was hit, it flew away.' '顿之' can be translated as 'when it was hit' or 'when it was struck.'
  • 帝为潘贵妃起神山、永寿二殿 - 'The emperor built the Shenshan and Yongshou palaces for his favorite consort Pan.' '起' means 'to build' or 'to construct.'
  • 皆饰以金壁 - 'Both were adorned with gold and jade.' '金壁' refers to gold and precious stones.
  • 内作飞仙帐 - 'Inside, there were flying fairy canopies.' '飞仙' refers to flying fairies or immortals.
  • 四回绣绮 - 'Four-sided embroidered silk curtains.' '绣绮' refers to embroidered silk.
  • 窗间尽画神仙 - 'The windows were all painted with depictions of immortals.'
  • 椽桷之端悉垂铃珮 - 'The ends of the rafters and beams were all hung with bells and pendants.'

  • 有古玉律数枚 - 'There were several ancient jade qin.' '玉律' refers to ancient jade qin (a type of musical instrument).
  • 悉裁以钿笛 - 'All were cut to make di (a type of flute).' '钿笛' refers to a type of flute.
  • 庄严寺有玉九子铃 - 'The Zhiyang Temple had a jade nine-son bell.' '九子铃' refers to a type of bell.
  • 外国寺佛面有光相 - 'The Buddha's face at the Foreign Temple had a radiant appearance.' '光相' refers to a radiant or glowing appearance.
  • 禅灵寺塔诸宝珥 - 'The Buddhist pagoda at the Chanling Temple had various precious ornaments.' '宝珥' refers to precious ornaments.
  • 皆剥取以施潘妃殿饰 - 'All were stripped off and used to decorate the palace of Consort Pan.' '剥取' means 'stripped off' or 'removed.'
  • 又凿为莲花以贴地 - 'He also carved lotus flowers to be placed on the ground.' '凿为' means 'carved into.'
  • 令潘妃行其上 - 'He made Consort Pan walk on it.' '行其上' means 'to walk on it.'
  • 涂壁皆以麝香 - 'The walls were coated with musk.' '涂壁' means 'coated the walls.'
  • 锦幔珠帘 - 'Silk curtains and pearl curtains.' '锦幔' refers to silk curtains, and '珠帘' refers to pearl curtains.
  • 穷极绮丽 - 'Reaching the utmost in beauty and splendor.'
  • 执役工匠 - 'Craftsmen and laborers.' '执役' means 'craftsmen and laborers.'
  • 自夜达晓 - 'From night until dawn.' '达晓' means 'until dawn.'
  • 犹不速副 - 'Still not fast enough to meet the deadline.' '速副' means 'fast enough to meet the deadline.'
  • 乃剔取诸佛寺刹殿藻 - 'So he stripped off the decorations from the pagodas and halls of the Buddhist temples.' '剔取' means 'stripped off.'
  • 并仙人骑兽以充之 - 'And used the carvings of immortals riding beasts to fill it.' '仙人骑兽' refers to carvings of immortals riding beasts.

  • 用贵市人间金银宝物 - 'He used expensive items from the market to buy gold, silver, and precious objects.' '贵市' means 'expensive market.'
  • 价皆数倍 - 'The prices were several times higher.' '数倍' means 'several times higher.'
  • 琥珀钗一只，值百七十万 - 'A single amber hairpin was worth one hundred and seventy million.' '值' means 'worth.'

  • 若得此，则我期老于其中也 - If only I could have such a place, I would spend my old age there. This expresses a wish for a private, secluded space.

  • 楼阁高下，轩窗掩映，幽房曲室，玉栏朱楯，互相连属，四合曲屋自通 - The towers and pavilions rise and fall, the windows and doors interweave, the secluded rooms and winding chambers, with jade railings and vermilion pillars, all connected and communicating freely. This vividly describes the intricate and luxurious design.
  • 千门万牖，上下金碧 - A thousand doors and ten thousand windows, adorned with gold and lapis lazuli. This emphasizes the opulence and grandeur of the structure.
  • 金虬伏于栋下，玉兽蹲于户旁 - Golden dragons crouch beneath the rafters, jade beasts sit beside the doors. This adds a mythical and decorative element.
  • 壁砌生光，琐窗射日，工巧之极，自古未有 - The walls and tiles emit a glow, the intricate windows catch the sunlight, the craftsmanship is exquisite beyond compare, unparalleled in history. This highlights the unparalleled beauty and skill.
  • 费用金玉，帑库为之一空 - The cost was immense, depleting the treasury. This indicates the financial impact of the project.
  • 人误入者，虽终日不能出 - Those who accidentally enter cannot find their way out even after a whole day. This suggests the complexity and maze-like nature of the structure.
  • 帝幸之，大喜，顾左右曰：“使其仙游其中，亦当自迷也，可目之曰‘迷楼’。” - The emperor visited it and was very pleased. He looked around and said, 'If one were to wander as a spirit within it, they would surely get lost. Let us call it the 'Labyrinth Palace'.' This shows the emperor's reaction and the naming of the palace.
  • 诏以五品官赐升 - He bestowed the rank of Fifth Class Official on Tang. This indicates the reward given to the architect.
  • 于迷楼上张四宝帐，帐各异名：一名“散春愁”，二名“醉忘归”，三名“夜酣香”，四名“延秋月”。 - He set up four precious tents on the Labyrinth Palace, each with a different name: 'Dispelling Spring Sorrows,' 'Drunk and Forgetful of Return,' 'Nightly Intoxication,' and 'Extending the Autumn Moon.' This describes the luxurious and evocative names of the tents.
  • 选良家女数千居楼中 - He selected thousands of noble women to live in the palace. This indicates the luxurious and exclusive nature of the palace.
  • 每一幸，或经月不出 - Each time he visited, he might stay for months without leaving. This emphasizes the emperor's prolonged stays.
  • 是月，大夫何稠进御童女车 - In that month, the official He Chou presented a chariot for the emperor's young concubines. This describes a specific event during the same period.

  • 虞世南 - A famous scholar and calligrapher of the Sui and Tang dynasties, known for his literary and artistic talents.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 五年三月上巳 - Specific date in the Chinese lunar calendar, marking the beginning of spring and a traditional festival.
  • 宴昭神亭 - Feast at the Bright God Pavilion, where *昭神亭* refers to a pavilion dedicated to a deity.
  • 妇女杂坐，夜分而罢 - Describes a mixed-gender gathering that ends at midnight.
  • 衍自执板 - Yan himself holds the board, where *执板* refers to holding a tablet used in musical performance.
  • 唱《霓裳羽衣》及《后庭花》、《思越人曲》 - Refers to singing three specific songs: *The Feathered Robe of Clouds*, *The Back Courtyard Flower*, and *Thoughts of a Man in Exile*.
  • 四月游浣花 - In April, they tour the Washing Flower (a scenic area).

  • 游人士女，珠翠夹岸 - Describes people and women adorned with jewels along the riverbank.
  • 日正午，暴风起，须臾，雷电晦冥 - Describes a sudden storm at noon, with lightning and darkness.
  • 有白鱼自江心跃出，变为蛟形，腾空而起 - Describes a white fish leaping from the river and transforming into a dragon-like creature, flying into the air.

  • 左张翠羽盖，曳金铃，结青锦为重云覆顶，旁建青龙旗，列磨锷雕银戟五 - Describes the left side of the carriage with a green feather canopy, golden bells, and five silver spears.
  • 右张白鸠缉毳盖，曳玉铃，结素锦为层云覆顶，旁建白虎旗，列豹绒连珠枪五 - Describes the right side with a white feather canopy, jade bells, and five leopard-fur spears.
  • 前张红猴毛毡盖，曳木铃，结赤锦为重云覆顶，前建朱雀旗，列金戈五 - Describes the front with a red monkey fur canopy, wooden bells, and five golden spears.
  • 后张黑兔团毫盖，曳竹铃，结墨锦为层云覆顶，后建玄武旗，列画干五 - Describes the back with a black rabbit fur canopy, bamboo bells, and five painted spears.
  • 中张雕羽曲柄盖，曳石铃，结黄锦为层云覆顶，建勾陈旗 - Describes the center with a carved feather canopy, stone bells, and a yellow flag.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 仲秋之夜 - Refers to the night of the Mid-Autumn Festival, a traditional Chinese holiday celebrated in the eighth month of the lunar calendar.
  • 泛月 - Literally 'floating on the moon,' a poetic expression describing the act of boating on the lake under the moonlight.
  • 画鹢中流 - '画鹢' refers to a boat decorated with a painting of a bird (likely a crane), and '中流' means 'in the middle of the stream.' This phrase describes the boat being in the center of the river.
  • 莲舟夹持 - '莲舟' refers to boats shaped like lotus leaves, and '夹持' means 'flanking' or 'supporting.' This phrase describes the lotus-shaped boats supporting the central boat.
  • 月丽中天 - '月丽' means 'moon shining brightly,' and '中天' means 'in the middle of the sky.' This phrase describes the moon shining brightly in the sky.
  • 彩云四合 - '彩云' means 'colored clouds,' and '四合' means 'coming together from all directions.' This phrase describes the colorful clouds gathering around the moon.
  • 开宴张乐 - '开宴' means 'to start a banquet,' and '张乐' means 'to play music.' This phrase describes the beginning of a feast and the playing of music.
  • 荐蜻翅之脯 - '荐' means 'to present,' '蜻翅' means 'dragonfly wings,' and '脯' means 'dried meat.' This phrase describes presenting dried meat shaped like dragonfly wings.
  • 进秋风之鲙 - '进' means 'to serve,' '秋风' means 'autumn wind,' and '鲙' means 'sliced fish.' This phrase describes serving sliced fish, possibly seasoned with autumn flavors.
  • 酌玄霜之酒 - '酌' means 'to pour,' '玄霜' means 'black frost,' and '酒' means 'wine.' This phrase describes pouring wine that is described as black and cold.
  • 啖华月之糕 - '啖' means 'to eat,' '华月' means 'beautiful moon,' and '糕' means 'cake.' This phrase describes eating a cake named after the beautiful moon.
  • 披罗曳縠 - '披罗' means 'wearing silk,' and '曳縠' means 'dragging gauze.' This phrase describes the women wearing light, flowing garments.
  • 贺新凉 - A song or poem titled '贺新凉' (Celebrating the New Coolness), likely referring to the coolness of the autumn season.
  • 步玄之声 - '步玄' means 'stepping into the void' or 'walking in the void,' and '声' means 'sound.' This phrase refers to the ethereal sounds made by the goddesses.
  • 月照临 - A dance or performance titled '月照临' (Moon Illuminating), likely referring to a dance that symbolizes the moon's illumination.

  • 瑶池 - A mythical pool in the West where the Queen Mother of the West (Xiwangmu) held banquets for immortals.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乘龙船泛月池上 - A poetic way to describe a leisurely boat ride, literally 'riding a dragon boat on the moonlit pool.'
  • 池起浮桥三处 - Means 'the pool has three floating bridges,' indicating the presence of multiple bridges over the pool.
  • 每处分三洞，洞上结彩为飞楼，楼上置女乐 - Describes the decoration of the bridges with three arches, each topped with a colorful pavilion, where female musicians are placed.
  • 桥以木为质，饰以锦绣，九洞不相直达 - Explains that the bridges are made of wood, decorated with brocade, and have nine arches that do not directly connect.
  • 令诸嫔妃祓于内园迎祥亭漾碧池 - Means 'the imperial consorts are ordered to perform purification rites in the inner garden at the Auspicious Pavilion by the Green Pool.'
  • 池用纹石为质，以宝石镂成，奇花繁叶，杂砌其间 - Describes the pool as lined with patterned stones and adorned with gemstones, with intricate flowers and leaves interspersed.
  • 上置紫云九龙华盖，四面施帏，帏皆蜀锦为之 - Explains that a purple cloud nine-dragon canopy is placed above, with curtains made of Shu brocade on all sides.
  • 跨池三桥，桥上结锦为亭 - Means 'there are three bridges crossing the pool, each with a pavilion made of brocade.'
  • 中匾集鸾，左匾凝霞，右匾承霄 - Describes the inscriptions on the pavilions: 'Gathering of Cranes' in the middle, 'Concentrated Clouds' on the left, and 'Receiving the Sky' on the right.
  • 祓毕，则宴饮于中，谓之“爽心宴” - Means 'after the purification rites, they feast in the middle, calling it the 'Refreshing Banquet.'
  • 池之旁有潭曰“香泉”，至此日，则积香水以注于池 - Explains that beside the pool is a spring called 'Fragrant Spring,' which on this day is used to fill the pool with scented water.
  • 池中又置温玉狻猊、白晶鹿、红石马等物 - Describes the pool as containing warm jade lion statues, white crystal deer, and red stone horses.
  • 嫔妃浴澡之余，则骑以为戏，或执兰蕙，或击球筑，谓之水上迎祥之乐 - Means 'the imperial consorts, after bathing, ride these figures as a game, sometimes holding orchids, sometimes playing ball, calling it the 'Waterborne Auspicious Celebration.'
  • 唯淑姬戈小娥体白而红，著水如桃花含露，愈争妍美 - Describes a particular concubine, Go Xiao'e, who is fair-skinned and rosy, appearing like a peach blossom with dew, more beautiful than others.
  • 此夭桃女也 - Means 'she is a young peach blossom girl,' a poetic way to describe her beauty.
  • 因呼为“蹇桃夫人”，宠爱有加焉 - Means 'thus she is called the 'Young Peach Blossom Lady' and is favored greatly.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 麗嫔張阿玄 - Refers to a beautiful concubine named Zhang A Xuan, with '麗嫔' being a title for a concubine.
  • 性號機敏 - Means 'naturally quick-witted' or 'naturally intelligent.'
  • 順帝或視朝退 - Means 'when Emperor Shun returns from the court session.'
  • 嬉遊後宮 - Means 'to play and frolic in the inner palace.'
  • 百歲光陰，等於馳電 - A metaphor comparing the passage of time to the speed of lightning, emphasizing its fleeting nature.
  • 日夜為樂，猶不滿十萬 - Means 'even if one enjoys day and night, it is still not enough for ten thousand days,' suggesting the brevity of life.
  • 如白雲有期，富貴皆非我有矣 - A metaphor comparing life to a passing cloud, implying that wealth and status are fleeting.
  • 何必自苦，虛度一生 - Means 'why should one suffer unnecessarily and waste one's life?'
  • 長歌大舞，自暮達旦 - Means 'to sing loudly and dance wildly from dusk until dawn,' describing a night of revelry.
  • 遣光 - A term coined by Zhang A Xuan, possibly meaning 'to send away the light' or 'to dispel the light,' symbolizing her philosophy of living life to the fullest.
  • 諸嬪貴妃，百媚其前，以求容悅 - Means 'the other concubines and noble consorts flatter her in every way to gain her favor.'
  • 私制一崑崙巾 - Means 'she privately made a K'unlun headscarf,' referring to a specific type of headwear.
  • 上起三層，中有樞轉 - Describes the headscarf as having three layers with a rotating mechanism.
  • 玉質金枝，紉彩為花，團綴於四面 - Means 'made of jade and gold, adorned with colorful flowers, and arranged around the four sides.'
  • 蜂蝶雜處其中 - Means 'bees and butterflies are mixed among the flowers.'
  • 行則三層摩運，百花自搖 - Means 'when she walks, the three layers rub against each other, causing the flowers to sway.'
  • 蜂蝶欲飛，皆作鑽蕊之狀 - Means 'the bees and butterflies seem to be about to fly, all in the posture of probing the flower buds.'

  • 帝見之，指謂眾嬪曰 - Means 'the emperor saw her and pointed to her, saying to the other concubines,' indicating his admiration for Zhang A Xuan.
  • 張嬪氣宇清越 - Means 'Zhang A Xuan has a clear and lofty aura,' describing her dignified and graceful demeanor.
  • 服帝子雲霓之服 - Means 'she wears a robe like the clouds and rainbows of the imperial son,' suggesting a divine or heavenly appearance.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 翻冠飛履之舞 - A specific type of dance where hats and shoes fly, indicating a high level of skill and performance.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宮人 - Refers to palace women, a common term in Chinese imperial history.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蟾光兮捉娥影 - Poetic expression referring to the moonlight and the reflection of the moon's goddess, Chang'e.
  • 露团团兮气清，风飕飕兮力劲 - Descriptive phrases that paint a picture of a clear night with dewdrops and a strong wind. '团团' (tuán tuán) for dewdrops suggests a round, full appearance.
  • 月一轮兮高且圆，华新发兮鲜复妍 - Describes the moon as high and round, with its light fresh and beautiful. '华新发兮鲜复妍' (huá xīn fā xī xiān fù yán) can be interpreted as the moonlight being newly emerged and both fresh and beautiful.
  • 愿万古兮每如此，予同乐兮终年 - An expression of a wish that the moon will always be as beautiful and that the joy it brings will last forever.
  • 帝复置酒于天香亭，为赏月饮 - Describes the emperor setting up a feast at the Tianxiang Pavilion for a moon-viewing party.
  • 香儿复易服趋亭前，为昂鸾缩鹤之舞 - Describes a girl named Xiang changing her clothes and dancing in front of the pavilion, performing a dance that mimics the movements of phoenixes and cranes.
  • 帝大悦，以为昔人《霓裳羽衣》不足逮也 - The emperor is very pleased, thinking that the dance surpasses the famous *Ní Cháng Yǔ Yī* (The Feathered Robe of the Celestial Maiden) dance.
  • 伽南楫兮文梓舟，泛波光兮远夷犹 - Describes a boat made of sandalwood and catalpa wood, gliding on the water's surface.
  • 波摇摇兮舟不定，扬予袂兮金风竞 - Describes the boat swaying gently on the waves, with the wind rustling the sleeves of the passengers.
  • 棹歌起兮纤手挥，青角脱兮水萦洄 - Describes the boatmen singing and the sound of the oars, with the water swirling around the boat.
  • 归去来兮乐更谁 - A rhetorical question expressing that the joy of the moment is unmatched.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蟾光 - Refers to the moonlight, often associated with the moon rabbit in Chinese mythology.
  • 娥影 - Refers to the shadow of Chang'e, the moon goddess.
  • 天香亭 - A pavilion where the emperor and his courtiers would gather for moon-viewing parties.
  • 昂鸾缩鹤之舞 - A type of dance that mimics the movements of phoenixes and cranes, often performed during festive occasions.
  • 《霓裳羽衣》 - A famous dance from the Tang Dynasty, known for its elegance and grace.
  • 玉泉山 - A mountain in Beijing, known for its scenic beauty and historical significance.
  • 吕公岩 - A rock formation on Yushan Mountain, named after the legendary figure Lü Gong.
  • 文梓 - Catalpa wood, known for its beauty and durability.
  • 伽南 - Sandalwood, known for its fragrance and use in incense and decorative items.

Proper Nouns

  • 蟾光 - Moonlight.
  • 娥影 - Shadow of Chang'e.
  • 天香亭 - Tianxiang Pavilion.
  • 昂鸾缩鹤之舞 - Dance of Phoenixes and Cranes.
  • 《霓裳羽衣》 - The Feathered Robe of the Celestial Maiden.
  • 玉泉山 - Yushan Mountain.
  • 吕公岩 - Lü Gong Rock.
  • 文梓 - Catalpa wood.
  • 伽南 - Sandalwood.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 河间王夜饮 - A straightforward description of a prince from the state of Hejian hosting a night drinking party.
  • 妓女讴歌 - Refers to female entertainers singing and performing during the banquet.
  • 一曲下一金牌 - A unique practice where a gold medal is given for each song performed.
  • 席终，金牌盈座 - By the end of the banquet, the table is filled with gold medals.
  • 使娼妓戴拂壶 - Means 'to have the courtesans wear a fan and a vase.'
  • 密粉淡妆 - Describes a light makeup application.
  • 奏书者号傅芳妓 - Refers to the female performers who read letters, called 'Fufang Jizi.'
  • 酌酒者号龙津女 - Refers to the female performers who serve wine, called 'Longjin Nu.'
  • 侍食者号仙盘使 - Refers to the female performers who serve food, called 'Xianpan Shi.'
  • 代书札者号墨蛾 - Refers to the female performers who write letters, called 'Moe.'
  • 接香者号龙姬 - Refers to the female performers who handle incense, called 'Longji.'
  • 总号凤窠群女 - Refers to all the female performers collectively, called 'Fengke Qunnu.'
  • 晋武帝尝降王武子家 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Jin visiting Wang Wuzi's home.
  • 武子供馔，并用琉璃器 - Wang Wuzi provided the meal using glassware.
  • 婢子百余人，皆绫罗袴褶，以手擎饮食 - Wang Wuzi had over a hundred maids in fine silk and trousers, serving the food by hand.
  • 杨国忠凡有客设酒，令妓女各执其事 - Yang Guozhong, when entertaining guests, had the courtesans perform various tasks.
  • 号肉台盘 - Refers to the courtesans serving food, called 'Rou Taipan.'
  • 冬月，令妓女围之，号肉屏风 - In winter, Yang Guozhong had the courtesans surround the guests, called 'Rou Pingfeng.'
  • 又选妾肥大者于前遮风，谓之肉障肉阵 - He also selected fat concubines to stand in front of the guests to block the wind, called 'Rou Zhang Rou Zhen.'
  • 孙晟每食，不设几案，使众姬各执一器环立 - Sun Cheng, when eating, did not set up tables and had the courtesans stand in a circle around him, each holding a dish.
  • 号肉台 - Refers to the courtesans standing in a circle, called 'Rou Tai.'
  • 杭州别驾杜驯，亦有肉屏风事 - Du Xun, the deputy governor of Hangzhou, also had the practice of using 'Rou Pingfeng.'
  • 岐王少惑女色 - Refers to the young Prince of Qi being seduced by women.
  • 每至冬寒，手冷不近火，惟纳于妓怀中，揣其肌肤，称为暖手 - During winter, when his hands were cold, he would put them in the courtesans' bosoms to warm them, calling it 'warm hands.'
  • 封陟家宴，使群婢各执一烛，四面行立，呼为烛围 - Feng Zhi, during a family banquet, had the maids each hold a candle and walk around, calling it 'Zhu Wei.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 河间王 - Refers to a prince from the state of Hejian during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 姑臧 - Refers to Guzang (present-day Wuwei, Gansu), a city during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 晋武帝 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Jin, the second emperor of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 王武子 - Refers to Wang Wuzi, a nobleman during the Jin Dynasty.
  • 杨国忠 - Refers to Yang Guozhong, a powerful minister during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 孙晟 - Refers to Sun Cheng, a nobleman during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 杭州 - Refers to Hangzhou, a city during the Tang Dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嚴世蕃吐唾，皆美婢以口承之 - Describes a luxurious and indulgent behavior where a beautiful maid catches the saliva of Yan Shifan with her mouth, which is then referred to as a '香唾盂' (fragrant spittoon).
  • 香唾盂 - A metaphorical term for the act described above, emphasizing the luxurious and sensual nature of the activity.
  • 雙陸圖 - A decorative pattern used on a screen or wall, derived from the board game 'Shuanglu.'
  • 肉雙陸 - A living version of the game 'Shuanglu,' where thirty-two beautiful women are dressed in half-black and half-white attire to represent the game pieces.
  • 新白綾汗巾 - New white silk handkerchiefs, which were often used to wipe sweat but are mentioned here in a context that suggests they were discarded after use.
  • 細骨輕軀 - A phrase used to describe a delicate and slender body, often associated with beauty and grace.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 嚴世蕃 - Yan Shifan was a prominent figure during the Ming Dynasty, known for his influence and corruption. He was the son of Yan Song, a powerful minister.
  • 王天華 - Wang Tianhua, a high-ranking official who was known for his flattery and servility towards Yan Shifan.
  • 石崇 - Shi Chong was a wealthy and influential figure during the Western Jin Dynasty, known for his extravagant lifestyle and debauchery.
  • 常舞 - A continuous dance performed by Shi Chong's concubines, which was a form of entertainment in his palace.
  • 沉水之香 - Sandalwood incense, a type of fragrant wood used in ancient China for its aromatic properties and spiritual significance.

Proper Nouns

  • 嚴世蕃 - Yan Shifan, a powerful and corrupt official during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 王天華 - Wang Tianhua, a high-ranking official who flattered Yan Shifan.
  • 石崇 - Shi Chong, a wealthy and influential figure during the Western Jin Dynasty.
  • 雙陸圖 - A decorative pattern used on a screen or wall, derived from the board game 'Shuanglu.'
  • 常舞 - A continuous dance performed by Shi Chong's concubines.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 元載末年 - Refers to the later years of Yuan Zai, a historical figure.
  • 納薛瑶英為姬 - Means 'took Xue Yaoying as a concubine.'
  • 處以金絲帳，卻塵褥，衣以龍绡衣 - Describes luxurious living conditions: placed in a silk-silver brocade tent, with a dust-free mattress, and dressed in a dragon-silk robe.
  • 載以瑶英體輕，不勝重衣，于異國求此服也 - Explains that Yuan Zai chose this attire because Yaoying's body was light and could not bear heavy clothing, and he sought it from a foreign land.
  • 至赠诗云 - Means 'Jia Zhi presented a poem saying.'
  • “舞怯珠衣重，笑疑桃脸开。方知汉武帝，虚筑避风台。” - A poem praising Yaoying's grace and beauty, comparing her to the legendary Han Emperor Wu who built a wind-avoiding pavilion.
  • 炎亦作长歌美之 - Means 'Yan also composed a long song to praise her.'
  • “雪面澹娥天上女，凤箫鸾翅欲飞去。玉钗碧翠步无尘，楚腰如柳不胜春。” - Another poem praising Yaoying's ethereal beauty and grace.
  • 宋祁先奉诏修《唐书》，既帅蜀，因以书局自随 - Describes Song Qi being commissioned to revise the *Tangshu* (History of the Tang Dynasty) and taking the editorial office with him when he became governor of Shu (Sichuan).
  • 每宴罢盥漱，辟寝门垂帘，燃一椽烛，媵婢夹侍，和墨伸纸 - Describes Song Qi's luxurious and private writing routine after banquets.
  • 望之者，知公修《唐书》，若神仙焉 - Means 'Those who saw him knew that he was revising the *Tangshu*, appearing like a fairy.'
  • 未成，顾诸姬曰：“汝辈俱曾在人家，曾见主人如此否？可谓清矣。”皆曰：“实无有。”其间一人，来自宗子家 - Song Qi asks his attendants if they had ever seen their masters behave so elegantly, and one of them, from a noble family, confirms that it was unprecedented.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元載 - Yuan Zai, a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 薛瑶英 - Xue Yaoying, a concubine of Yuan Zai, known for her beauty and grace.
  • 贾至 - Jia Zhi, a poet and official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 杨公雅 - Yang Gongya, another poet and official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 宋祁 - Song Qi, a historian and poet during the Song Dynasty.
  • 《唐书》 - The *Tangshu* (History of the Tang Dynasty), a historical record of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 澄心堂纸 - A high-quality paper produced in the Tang Dynasty, known for its fine texture and durability.
  • 宗子家 - Refers to a noble family, indicating a person of high social status.

  • 贾至 - Jia Zhi
  • 杨公雅 - Yang Gongya
  • 宋祁 - Song Qi
  • 《唐书》 - *Tangshu* (History of the Tang Dynasty)
  • 澄心堂纸 - High-quality paper from the Tang Dynasty

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 汝太尉 - Formal address to a high-ranking official; 'You, the Taifu'.
  • 拥炉列酒撰 - Surrounded by a brazier, with wine and delicacies arranged; describes a luxurious setting.
  • 罗管弦 - With musical instruments arranged; refers to a musical performance.
  • 歌舞之余 - After dancing and singing; indicates a leisurely activity.
  • 间以杂剧 - Interspersed with various plays; refers to theatrical performances.
  • 引满大醉 - Drink to the brim and get drunk; describes heavy drinking.
  • 不能为尚书清事也 - Cannot engage in the clear and pure affairs of a Shangshu; contrasts luxurious lifestyle with official duties.
  • 不晓天 - Not knowing the sky; metaphor for not understanding the passage of time or the natural order.
  • 寄语学士 - Deliver a message to the scholar-official; formal address.
  • 吃齑煮饭 - Eating a simple meal of boiled vegetables and rice; humble and frugal meal.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宋 - Refers to Song Qi, a high-ranking official during the Song Dynasty.
  • 太尉 - High-ranking military and political title in ancient China, equivalent to a prime minister.
  • 尚书 - High-ranking official title, often referring to the head of a department in the imperial government.
  • 子京 - Nickname for Song Qi, indicating his close relationship with the emperor.
  • 上元夜 - The Lantern Festival, celebrated on the 15th day of the first lunar month.
  • 书院 - Traditional Chinese educational institution, often associated with scholars and intellectuals.
  • 《周易》 - The Book of Changes, one of the Five Classics of Chinese literature and philosophy.
  • 点华灯 - Lighting up with colorful lamps; describes a festive and luxurious setting.
  • 歌妓 - Female entertainers who sang and danced, often employed in wealthy households or public performances.

Proper Nouns

  • 尚书 - High-ranking official title.
  • 子京 - Nickname for Song Qi.

  • 书院 - Traditional Chinese educational institution.
  • 《周易》 - The Book of Changes.
  • 相公 - Respectful term for a high-ranking official, often used to address Song Qi.
  • 学士 - Scholar-official, often referring to someone with a high level of education and status.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 按《蜀广记》故事 - Introduces a reference to a historical or legendary account from *Shu Guang Ji*, a text that may not be widely known.
  • 固见宋公风流 - Refers to the elegance of Song Gong; '风流' can mean both elegance and libertine behavior, depending on context.
  • 河东狮吼 - A well-known idiom referring to a wife who dominates her husband, derived from a story about Chen Zao (陈慥).
  • 政从豪华一番 - '政从' can be translated as 'precisely from' or 'exactly from'; '豪华' means luxury and splendor.
  • 尝过滋味 - A metaphorical expression indicating someone has lived through certain experiences.
  • 回头猛省 - A phrase that conveys a moment of epiphany or self-awareness.
  • 轮台之悔 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Han's decision to abandon the campaign in Luntai due to its failure, symbolizing a moment of self-criticism.
  • 汉武轮台之悔 - Specific historical reference to Emperor Wu of Han's regret at Luntai.
  • 所用刀六十余斤 - '斤' is an old unit of weight, equivalent to about 0.5 kilograms.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《蜀广记》 - A historical or legendary text, possibly a local chronicle or anthology, that records stories about Sichuan (Shu).
  • 太守 - Refers to the governor or magistrate of a prefecture, a high-ranking official in ancient China.
  • 大慈寺 - A Buddhist temple, likely located in Chengdu or another major city in Sichuan.
  • 宋公祁 - Refers to Song Qi (宋祁), a famous poet and scholar of the Northern Song dynasty.
  • 临邛周之纯 - Refers to Zhou Zhichun, a talented lyricist from Linqiong (modern-day Leshan, Sichuan).
  • 河东狮吼 - A famous idiom that refers to a story about Chen Zao (陈慥), who was known for his wife's dominance over him.
  • 刘大刀 - Refers to Liu Ting (刘烶), a famous general during the reign of Emperor Shenzong of the Song dynasty.
  • 燕、赵之选 - Refers to the regions of Yan and Zhao, both historically known for their martial culture and beauty.
  • 轮台之悔 - Refers to a historical event in which Emperor Wu of Han abandoned his military campaigns in the Western Regions.

Proper Nouns

  • 《蜀广记》 - A historical or legendary text, possibly a local chronicle or anthology, that records stories about Sichuan (Shu).
  • 太守 - The governor or magistrate of a prefecture.
  • 大慈寺 - A Buddhist temple, likely located in Chengdu or another major city in Sichuan.
  • 宋公祁 - Song Qi (宋祁), a famous poet and scholar of the Northern Song dynasty.
  • 临邛周之纯 - Zhou Zhichun, a talented lyricist from Linqiong (modern-day Leshan, Sichuan).
  • 河东狮吼 - A famous idiom that refers to a story about Chen Zao (陈慥).
  • 刘大刀 - Liu Ting (刘烶), a famous general during the reign of Emperor Shenzong of the Song dynasty.
  • 燕、赵之选 - Concubines chosen from the regions of Yan and Zhao, known for their martial culture and beauty.
  • 轮台之悔 - A historical event in which Emperor Wu of Han abandoned his military campaigns in the Western Regions.
  • 汉武轮台之悔 - Emperor Wu of Han's regret at Luntai, a specific historical reference to his decision to abandon his military campaigns.

[333]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太常卿 - High-ranking official in the imperial court, responsible for rituals and ceremonies.
  • 王相国 - Refers to a high-ranking official or minister, here specifically the father of the protagonist.
  • 额有疤痕，号三只眼 - Metaphorical description indicating a scar on the forehead, often symbolizing a unique or divine mark.
  • 廉介不染 - Means 'upright and incorruptible,' describing a person of high moral integrity.
  • 善于经营 - Means 'skilled in managing affairs' or 'good at business.'
  • 久之富甲一郡 - Means 'eventually became the richest in the region,' emphasizing the accumulation of wealth over time.
  • 晚年益豪奢自喜 - Means 'in his later years, he became increasingly extravagant and proud of it.'
  • 宠姬数十人 - Means 'dozens of favored concubines,' indicating a large number of favored women.
  • 人设一院，左右鳞次而居 - Means 'each had a separate courtyard, arranged in a fish-scale pattern,' describing the layout of the courtyards.
  • 夜则悬纱灯其上，照耀如昼 - Means 'at night, they hung silk lamps above, illuminating the area like daylight,' describing the lighting in the courtyards.
  • 每姬择洁秀婢二人侍之 - Means 'each concubine chose two clean and beautiful maids to serve her,' indicating the selection of maids for each concubine.
  • 姬行，则一婢随，一婢居守 - Means 'when the concubine went out, one maid followed, and one stayed behind,' describing the arrangement of maids.
  • 每夜必开宴，老夫妇居中，诸姬列坐 - Means 'every night, a banquet was held, with the elderly couple in the center and all the concubines seated around them,' describing the seating arrangement during banquets.
  • 女乐献技，诸姬以次上寿 - Means 'female musicians performed, and the concubines took turns offering toasts,' describing the entertainment and toasting during banquets.
  • 爵三行，乐阕，夫人避席去 - Means 'after three rounds of toasts, the music stopped, and the lady left her seat,' describing the sequence of events during the banquet.
  • 太常乃与诸姬纵饮为乐 - Means 'then the Grand Master drank freely with the concubines, enjoying himself,' describing the Grand Master's behavior after the banquet.
  • 最后出白玉卮进酒 - Means 'finally, he presented a white jade cup filled with wine,' describing the presentation of the special cup.
  • 此卮莹洁无瑕，制极精巧，云是汉物，权贵所献 - Means 'this cup was as pure and flawless as a jade, extremely delicate and exquisite, said to be a Han dynasty object, presented by a powerful noble,' describing the characteristics and origin of the cup.
  • 太常宝惜，不轻及人 - Means 'the Grand Master treasured it and rarely showed it to others,' describing his attitude towards the cup.
  • 唯是夜所属意者，则酌以赐焉 - Means 'only the person he favored that night would be given a drink from it,' describing the special treatment of the favored person.
  • 婢视卮到处，还报本院 - Means 'the maids would report back to their own courtyard when the cup was passed around,' describing the maids' actions.
  • 院婢庀榼温酒以待 - Means 'maids in the courtyard prepared jugs of warm wine to wait,' describing the preparation for the next round of drinks.
  • 房老掌灯来迎，诸姬拥入院，始散去 - Means 'the old servant holding a lamp came to greet them, and the concubines entered the courtyard, finally dispersing,' describing the final parting of the concubines.
  • 余纱灯俱熄，惟本院存 - Means 'all the other silk lamps were extinguished, leaving only the lamp in the favored courtyard,' describing the remaining light.
  • 各院望见竿灯未息，知尚私饮未寝，啧啧相羡叹 - Means 'the other courtyards saw the pole lamp still lit and knew the Grand Master was still privately drinking, envying and sighing,' describing the reaction of the other courtyards.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太常卿 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court, responsible for rituals and ceremonies, often associated with the highest levels of government.
  • 王相国 - Refers to a high-ranking official or minister, often a powerful figure in the government.
  • 额有疤痕，号三只眼 - A metaphorical description often used to indicate a unique or divine mark, possibly suggesting a special status or destiny.
  • 廉介不染 - A term used to describe a person of high moral integrity, often associated with officials who are upright and incorruptible.
  • 善于经营 - A skill often associated with successful businesspeople or administrators.
  • 久之富甲一郡 - A phrase indicating the accumulation of wealth over time, often used to describe the success of a family or individual.
  • 晚年益豪奢自喜 - A description of a person becoming increasingly extravagant and proud in their later years, often associated with a decline in moral character.
  • 宠姬数十人 - A common practice among wealthy and powerful men, indicating a large number of favored concubines.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 娇如岂真欲得锦帐三十重哉？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'Is Jiao Ru truly desiring thirty layers of brocade curtains?' used to express doubt or disbelief.
  • 特以试其诚耳！ - Means 'It is just to test her sincerity!' emphasizes the purpose of the action.
  • 惟言莫违，所需必办。 - Means 'Only do not contradict her words, and whatever she needs will be provided.' indicates a promise or commitment.
  • 娇如不归柳，何归乎？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'If Jiao Ru does not return to Liu, to whom else will she return?' expresses the exclusivity of her relationship.
  • 唐玄宗在民间，闻娇如之名。 - Means 'Emperor Xuanzong was in the common people when he heard of Jiao Ru's name.' sets the historical context.
  • 及即位，召入宫。 - Means 'Upon ascending the throne, he summoned her into the palace.' describes the sequence of events.
  • 娇如见上涕泣，称痼疾且老。 - Means 'Jiao Ru saw the emperor and wept, claiming a chronic illness and old age.' describes her reaction and excuse.
  • 上知其不欲背柳氏，乃许其归。 - Means 'The emperor knew that she did not wish to leave Liu, so he allowed her to return.' explains the emperor's decision.
  • 卓哉志节，亦青楼中之以品胜者乎！ - Means 'Truly noble is her loyalty and integrity! Is she not the one who excels in character among the courtesans?' praises her character.
  • 寿王妃当愧死矣。 - Means 'The wife of Prince Shou should be ashamed to die!' expresses a strong sentiment of disapproval.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 柳 - Refers to Liu, likely a nobleman or official with whom Jiao Ru had a close relationship.
  • 唐玄宗 - Refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a prominent emperor known for his cultural achievements and the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 青楼 - Refers to brothels or the world of courtesans, often associated with high culture and artistic talent.
  • 寿王妃 - Refers to the wife of Prince Shou, a member of the imperial family.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 娇如岂真欲得锦帐三十重哉？特以试其诚耳！ - This rhetorical question and its answer form a single unit, emphasizing the purpose of the action.
  • 惟言莫违，所需必办。 - This phrase is a conditional statement that should be read as a single unit.
  • 娇如不归柳，何归乎？ - This rhetorical question should be read as a single unit, emphasizing the exclusivity of her relationship.
  • 唐玄宗在民间，闻娇如之名。及即位，召入宫。 - This sequence of events should be read as a single narrative unit.
  • 娇如见上涕泣，称痼疾且老。上知其不欲背柳氏，乃许其归。 - This sequence of actions and reactions should be read as a single narrative unit.
  • 卓哉志节，亦青楼中之以品胜者乎！寿王妃当愧死矣。 - This praise and condemnation should be read as a single unit, emphasizing the moral judgment.

Proper Nouns

  • 柳 - The name of the nobleman or official.
  • 唐玄宗 - The name of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 寿王妃 - The name of the wife of Prince Shou.

  • 相见惊悲 - The emotional reaction of the protagonist and the woman upon seeing each other after the supernatural events highlights the human element of the story.
  • 娶女为妇 - The marriage of the protagonist and the woman is a resolution to the story, bringing closure to the supernatural events.

  • 私附紅箋進上曰 - '私附' means to attach privately, '紅箋' is red paper, and '進上' means to present to the emperor.
  • 驛騎傳雙果，君王寵念深 - '驛騎' refers to a messenger on horseback, '傳雙果' means to deliver a pair of fruits, and '寵念深' means deep favor and care.
  • 爭知辭帝里，無復合歡心 - '爭知' means how could one know, '辭帝里' means to leave the imperial city, and '無復合歡心' means there is no longer any joy.
  • 時越溪進耀光綾 - '越溪' is a place name, and '耀光綾' is a type of silk.
  • 綾文突起，有光彩 - '綾文' refers to the pattern on the silk, '突起' means raised, and '有光彩' means it has a lustrous appearance.
  • 繰絲女夜夢神人告之 - '繰絲女' refers to a silk spinner, '夜夢' means to dream at night, and '神人' means a divine being.
  • 織成，果符所夢，故進之 - '織成' means to weave, '果符所夢' means it indeed matches the dream, and '故進之' means so it was presented.
  • 帝獨賜司花女洎絳仙，他姬莫預 - '獨賜' means to grant exclusively, '司花女' refers to a flower girl, '洎' means including, and '他姬莫預' means other concubines did not participate.
  • 卓文君姣好，眉色如望遠山，臉際常若芙蓉，肌膚柔滑如脂 - '姣好' means beautiful, '眉色如望遠山' means her eyebrows are as dark as distant mountains, '臉際常若芙蓉' means her face is often like a lotus flower, and '肌膚柔滑如脂' means her skin is soft and smooth like fat.
  • 為人放誕風流，故悅長卿之才而越禮焉 - '放誕風流' means unrestrained and elegant, '故悅長卿之才而越禮焉' means she was attracted to Zhang Heng's talent and went beyond proper etiquette.
  • 作《美人賦》欲以自刺，而終不能改，卒以此疾至死 - '作《美人賦》' means he wrote the 'Beauty Ode,' '欲以自刺' means to criticize himself, '而終不能改' means but he could not change, and '卒以此疾至死' means he ultimately died from this disease.
