Idiomatic Expressions

  • 杀马 - Literally 'to kill a horse,' but in the context of *The Romance of the Western Chamber*, it refers to a story where a man sacrifices his prized horse to win the favor of a woman. This idiom is used metaphorically to describe excessive devotion or sacrifice for love.
  • 元鼎情痴之名，遂为所掩 - A literary allusion to Yuan Dang (元鼎), a character known for his passionate love, but whose fame is overshadowed by the story of the horse sacrifice.
  • 五花名马价无伦 - Refers to the highest quality horses, often used as a metaphor for something extremely valuable.
  • 解道贵人而贱畜 - Literally 'understanding that humans are valuable but animals are lowly,' but in this context, it criticizes the idea that humans should be valued more than animals.
  • 一心无计博馀欢 - Means 'with one heart, unable to find a way to win extra joy,' describing someone who is desperate for love or affection.
  • 不知何策脍人肝 - Literally 'does not know what strategy to cut human livers,' but metaphorically means that someone is so consumed by passion that they are willing to do anything, even extreme or harmful actions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《绣襦记》 - A famous Chinese play that tells the story of a man who sacrifices his horse to win the love of a woman. The play is part of the *Romance of the Western Chamber* tradition, which is a collection of stories about love and romance.
  • 郑元和 - A character in *The Romance of the Western Chamber* who is known for his passionate love for a courtesan. His story is often cited as an example of romantic devotion.
  • 龙子犹 - A poet who wrote four quatrains about the story of the horse sacrifice. His work is part of the classical Chinese poetry tradition.
  • 阮佃夫 - A court official during the Southern Song Dynasty who was favored by Emperor Ming. He is mentioned in the text as a character who sought to acquire a beautiful courtesan.
  • 庐江何恢 - A historical figure who owned a beautiful courtesan named Zhang Yaohua. He refused to give her up, even when pressured by a powerful official.
  • 张耀华 - A courtesan who was the object of desire for both Zhu Danfu and Emperor Ming. Her story highlights the tension between personal loyalty and political power.
  • 宋阮佃夫 - A court official who sought to acquire Zhang Yaohua from He Hui, the owner of the courtesan. His attempt to pressure He Hui through official channels is a historical event.
  • 明帝 - Refers to Emperor Ming of the Southern Song Dynasty, who was known for his favoritism towards certain court officials.
  • 广州刺史 - A high-ranking official in the Southern Song Dynasty who was appointed to govern the region of Guangzhou.
  • 讽有司以公事弹恢 - A political maneuver where an official uses public office to accuse or criticize another person. In this case, Zhu Danfu uses this tactic to try to acquire Zhang Yaohua from He Hui.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 內枢密使 - High-ranking official in the imperial court, responsible for military affairs and political decision-making.
  • 有器量 - Describes someone with a broad and generous disposition, able to handle various situations with grace.
  • 家人未尝见其喜怒 - Indicates that the person's family never witnessed his expressions of joy or anger, suggesting a calm and composed demeanor.
  • 嬖于美妾解愁 - Means he was deeply fond of his beautiful concubine named Jiechou, which eventually led to his illness.
  • 妾姓赵氏，其母梦吞海棠花蕊而生 - A folkloric explanation of the concubine's birth, indicating a mystical or auspicious origin.
  • 颇有国色 - Describes the concubine as exceptionally beautiful, comparable to the beauty of a nation.
  • 善为新声，及工小词 - Indicates that she was skilled in composing new songs and was adept at writing short poems.
  • 蜀王建尝至炕第，见之，谓曰 - Describes an encounter between the king of Shu and Pan Kang, where the king expressed his admiration for the concubine.
  • 此臣下贱人，不敢以荐于君 - Pan Kang's polite refusal, emphasizing the concubine's low status and his unwillingness to offer her to the king.
  • 其实靳之 - Suggests that Pan Kang was actually being stingy or reluctant to part with her.
  • 绿珠之祸，可不戒耶 - Refers to the tragic fate of Lu Zhu, a concubine who was killed by bandits, warning against the dangers of such attachments.
  • 人生贵适意，岂能爱死，而自不足于心哉 - A philosophical statement about the value of personal satisfaction and the futility of self-denial.
  • 人皆服其有守 - Indicates that people admired Pan Kang for his steadfastness and integrity.
  • 何恢之惜耀华，潘炕之惜解愁，与石崇之惜绿珠，一辙耳 - A comparison of different historical figures who were similarly attached to their concubines.
  • 死生荣辱命也，出妻献妾，于以求免，去死几何 - A reflection on the inevitability of fate and the moral implications of giving up one's wife or concubine to avoid death.
  • 恢、炕之义为正矣 - Acknowledges the moral righteousness of He Hui and Pan Kang.
  • 即崇之辞孙秀，吾犹取之 - Expresses approval of Shi Chong's refusal to give up his concubine to Sun Xiu.

  • 石崇 - A wealthy and influential official during the Western Jin dynasty, known for his opulence and the tragic fate of his concubine Lu Zhu.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 順帝宮嬪進御無紀 - The consorts of Emperor Shun entered the palace without order, indicating a lack of regulation or discipline in the imperial harem.
  • 佩夫人、貴妃印者不下百數 - Those who wore the seals of Madame and Noble Consort numbered no less than a hundred, indicating a large number of consorts.
  • 所好成之，所惡除之 - Those she favored were promoted, those she disliked were removed, indicating the influence of the consorts over the emperor.
  • 位在皇后之下，而權則重於禁闱 - Her position was below that of the Empress, but her power was greater than that within the inner palace, indicating the significant influence of the consorts despite their lower rank.
  • 宮中稱為七貴云 - They were called the Seven Noble Ones in the palace, indicating the status and influence of the consorts.
  • 坡公之谪惠州也 - When the Master Su was exiled to Huizhou, referring to Su Shi's exile.
  • 惠有温都监女，颇有色，年十六，不肯嫁人 - In Huizhou, there was the daughter of Inspector Wen, who was quite beautiful, sixteen years old, and unwilling to marry, describing the consort's appearance and status.
  • 闻坡公至，甚喜，谓人曰：“此吾婿也。” - When she heard that the Master Su had arrived, she was very happy and told people, 'This is my husband,' indicating her affection for Su Shi.
  • 每夜闻坡讽咏，则徘徊窗外 - Every night when she heard the Master Su reciting poems, she would linger outside the window, indicating her admiration for Su Shi's poetry.
  • 坡觉而推窗，则其女逾墙而去 - The Master Su woke up and pushed the window open, and she had already jumped over the wall and gone, indicating her elusiveness.
  • 坡从而物色之，温具言其然 - The Master Su then sought her out, and Inspector Wen fully explained the situation, indicating Su Shi's attempt to find the consort.
  • 吾当呼王郎与子为姻 - I will call Wang Lang to marry you, indicating Su Shi's intention to arrange a marriage.
  • 及坡回惠日，其女已死，葬沙滩之侧矣 - When the Master Su returned to Huizhou, the girl had already died and was buried beside the sandbank, indicating the tragic end of the story.
  • 坡怅然赋《孤鸿》，调寄《卜算子》云 - The Master Su sadly composed the poem 'The Lonely Wild Goose,' set to the tune of 'The卜算子,' indicating Su Shi's emotional response to the event.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 缺月挂疏桐 - Poetic image of a crescent moon hanging over sparse mulberry trees, evoking solitude and melancholy.
  • 漏断人初静 - '漏' refers to an ancient water clock, and '断' means 'stopped.' Describes the deep night when the clock has stopped and people have fallen silent.
  • 时见幽人独往来 - '幽人' refers to a reclusive or solitary person, and '独往来' means 'moving alone.' Describes the solitary wanderings of such a person.
  • 缥渺孤鸿影 - '缥渺' means 'faint' or 'ethereal,' and '孤鸿影' refers to the shadow of a solitary wild goose. Evokes a sense of loneliness and transience.
  • 拣尽寒枝不肯栖 - '拣尽' means 'to choose through,' '寒枝' refers to cold branches, and '不肯栖' means 'refusing to rest.' Describes the wild goose's refusal to rest on any of the cold branches.
  • 寂寞沙洲冷 - '寂寞' means 'lonely,' '沙洲' refers to a sandbar, and '冷' means 'cold.' Describes the cold and lonely state of the sandbar.
  • 借鸿为喻 - '借鸿为喻' means 'using the wild goose as a metaphor,' indicating that the wild goose is used symbolically rather than literally.
  • 属意王氏女 - '属意' means 'to have affection for,' and '王氏女' refers to a woman from the Wang family. Indicates that the poet had affection for a woman from the Wang family.
  • 婆娑六十老人矣 - '婆娑' means 'to sway or dance,' and '六十老人矣' means 'a sixty-year-old man.' Describes the poet as a sixty-year-old man.
  • 何喜乎而心许之 - '何喜乎' means 'what joy,' and '心许之' means 'to promise in one's heart.' Indicates that the poet promised something in his heart due to joy.
  • 少择偶不嫁 - '少择偶' means 'to choose a mate sparingly,' and '不嫁' means 'not to marry.' Indicates that the woman chose her mate sparingly and did not marry.
  • 即呼王郎为姻 - '即呼' means 'to call,' '王郎' refers to a man from the Wang family, and '为姻' means 'as a relative.' Indicates that the woman would call a man from the Wang family as a relative.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 惠州 - City in Guangdong Province, where Su Shi was exiled.
  • 黄州 - City in Hubei Province, where Su Shi was also exiled.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少游乃戲曰 - Ouyang Xiu (欧阳修) says in a playful manner.
  • 若愛秦學士，徒悅其辭耳 - If you love Qin Guan (秦观), it is only because of his words.
  • 使親見其貌，未必然也 - Even if you see his appearance, it may not necessarily be so.
  • 妓大驚，色若不怿者 - The courtesan is greatly surprised, and her expression seems displeased.
  • 稍稍引退，入告母媪 - Gradually withdraw and inform the old lady.
  • 媪出設位，坐少游於堂 - The old lady comes out and sets up a place for Ouyang Xiu in the hall.
  • 妓冠帔立階下，北面拜 - The courtesan stands at the bottom of the steps, dressed in a headdress and cloak, and bows facing north.
  • 少游起且避 - Ouyang Xiu rises and avoids the situation.
  • 媪掖之坐，以受拜 - The old lady helps the courtesan sit to receive the bow.
  • 已乃張筵，飲虛左席，示不敢抗 - Then set up a banquet, drink from the empty left seat, showing that he dare not resist.
  • 母子左右侍觞 - The mother and daughter serve wine on both sides.
  • 酒一行，率歌少游词一阕以侑之 - One cup of wine, usually sing one stanza of Ouyang Xiu's lyrics to accompany the drinking.
  • 止少游宿 - Ouyang Xiu stays overnight.
  • 衾枕席褥，必躬设 - Bedding, must be personally arranged.
  • 夜分寝定，妓乃寝 - Midnight, settled to sleep, the courtesan then sleeps.
  • 平明先起，饰冠帔，奉沃匜，立帐外以俟 - At dawn, get up first, dress in a headdress and cloak, carry water for washing, stand outside the tent and wait.
  • 少游感其意，为留数日 - Ouyang Xiu is moved by her feelings and stays for several days.
  • 妓不敢以燕情见，愈加敬礼 - The courtesan does not express intimate feelings and increases in respect.
  • 将别，嘱曰 - Before parting, entrust and say.
  • 妾不肖之身，幸侍左右 - I, an unworthy person, am fortunate to serve by your side.
  • 今学士以王命不可久留，妾恐贻累，又不敢从行，惟誓洁身以报 - Now the scholar cannot stay long due to imperial orders, I fear to bring trouble, also dare not follow, only swear to remain pure to repay.
  • 他日北归，幸一过妾，妾愿毕矣 - One day return north, gracefully visit me once, my wish will be fulfilled.
  • 少游许之 - Ouyang Xiu agreed to it.

  • 媪 - Refers to an elderly woman, likely the mother of the courtesan.
  • 北面拜 - A traditional gesture of respect, where one bows facing north.
  • 虚左席 - A seat left empty, often reserved for a guest of honor.
  • 燕情 - Intimate feelings or emotions, often used in the context of romantic or close personal relationships.

Proper Nouns

  • 秦學士 - Qin Guan (秦观).
  • 妓 - Courtesan.
  • 媪 - Old lady.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一别数年 - Common expression meaning 'after a few years of separation.'
  • 竟死于藤 - 'Finally died in Teng,' where '竟' implies an unexpected or unfortunate end.
  • 闭门谢客 - Phrase meaning 'shut the door and refuse guests,' indicating seclusion.
  • 誓不以此身负少游也 - 'Swore not to betray Shao You with this body,' expressing a vow of loyalty.
  • 昼寝寤 - 'Awake from a daytime nap,' where '寤' means to awaken.
  • 非吉兆也 - 'Not a good omen,' indicating a negative prediction.
  • 亟遣仆沿途觇之 - 'Hastily sent servants to inquire along the way,' showing urgency and action.
  • 衰服以赴 - 'Went in mourning attire,' indicating deep sorrow.
  • 拊棺绕之三周 - 'Touched the coffin and circled it three times,' a traditional gesture of mourning.
  • 举声一恸而绝 - 'Raised her voice in a single cry and died,' indicating a sudden and intense emotional response.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 千古女子中愛才者 - Formal expression meaning 'throughout history, women who love talent.'
  • 風塵浪宕之質 - Describes a life of debauchery and indulgence, often associated with courtesans.
  • 執婦道終身 - Means 'to adhere to the role of a wife for life.'
  • 雖曰貞妓可也 - A concession that while she may be considered a virtuous courtesan, it is still remarkable.
  • 傳食妓館 - Means 'to eat at the expense of courtesans,' implying a life of dependency on them.
  • 醵錢葬之樂游原上 - Refers to a burial ceremony on Le You Yuan, where Liu Yong's followers gathered to mourn him.
  • 每春日踏青 - Means 'every spring during outings.'
  • 爭以酒酹之 - Means 'to compete in pouring wine as an offering.'
  • 吊柳七 - A ritual of mourning Liu Yong, known as 'Liu Qi' (柳七).
  • 委身 - Means 'to dedicate oneself' or 'to marry.'
  • 非真爱才者也 - Means 'not truly a lover of talent.'
  • 子猶氏曰 - Refers to the commentator 'Ziyou,' who provides commentary.
  • 白傅與李贊皇不協 - Refers to the conflict between Bai Juyi (白居易) and Li Zanhuang (李贊皇).
  • 見词翰则回吾心矣 - Means 'seeing his writings would turn my heart.'
  • 王巧兒歌舞顏色稱於京師 - Describes Wang Qiao (王巧兒) as a famous dancer and singer in the capital.
  • 陳雲峤與之狎 - Means 'Chen Yunqiao (陳雲峤) had a close relationship with her.'
  • 王欲嫁之 - Means 'Wang Qiao wanted to marry him.'
  • 其母密遣其流辈開喻 - Means 'her mother secretly sent her peers to persuade her.'
  • 陳公之妻，乃鐵太師女 - Refers to Chen Yunqiao's wife, who was the daughter of Iron Grand Tutor (鐵太師).
  • 妒悍不可言 - Means 'jealous and fierce, beyond words.'
  • 爾若歸其家，必遭凌辱矣 - Means 'if you marry into his family, you will surely suffer abuse.'
  • 巧儿一贱娼 - Means 'Wang Qiao, a lowly courtesan.'
  • 蒙陳公厚眷，得侍巾栉 - Means 'to receive Chen Yunqiao's favor and serve him closely.'
  • 雖死无憾 - Means 'even if I die, I will have no regrets.'
  • 母知其志不可夺 - Means 'her mother knew her resolve could not be changed.'
  • 潜挈家僻所 - Means 'secretly moved her family to a remote place.'
  • 陈不知也 - Means 'Chen Yunqiao did not know.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 旬日后 - Temporal expression meaning 'ten days later.'
  • 母氏设计置我某所 - Complex phrase meaning 'my mother devised a plan to place me in a certain place.' The word '设计' means 'devised' or 'planned,' and '置' means 'to place.'
  • 有富商约某日来 - Means 'there is a wealthy merchant who will come on a certain day.' The word '约' means 'to agree' or 'to arrange.'
  • 君当图之 - Means 'you should take action against him.' The word '图' here means 'to plan' or 'to take action.'
  • 恐无及矣 - Means 'it will be too late if we don't act now.' The phrase '无及矣' conveys the urgency of the situation.
  • 饮至夜分 - Means 'drank until midnight.' The word '夜分' refers to midnight.
  • 抚其肌肤皆损 - Means 'touched his body and found it all damaged.' The phrase '肌肤皆损' describes the condition of the body.
  • 遂不及乱 - Means 'thus did not proceed to sexual intercourse.' The word '乱' here refers to sexual intercourse.
  • 五鼓 - Time expression meaning 'five drums' or 'dawn.' In ancient China, the night was divided into five watches, each marked by the beating of drums.
  • 宿构忽剌罕赤挞搏商 - Complex phrase meaning 'prepared a sudden attack with a fierce blow to the merchant.' The words '宿构' mean 'prepared,' '忽剌罕赤' is a term for a fierce blow, and '挞搏' means 'to attack' or 'to strike.'
  • 欲赴刑部处置 - Means 'wanted to take him to the Ministry of Punishment for punishment.' The phrase '处置' means 'to deal with' or 'to punish.'
  • 告陈公曰 - Means 'told Master Chen.' The word '告' means 'to tell' or 'to inform.'
  • 某初不知 - Means 'I initially did not know.' The word '某' is a humble self-reference.
  • 幸寝其事 - Means 'please forgive me for this matter.' The phrase '寝其事' means 'to let it rest' or 'to forgive.'
  • 愿献钱二百缗 - Means 'I wish to offer two hundred strings of cash.' The word '缗' refers to a string of coins.
  • 以助财礼之费 - Means 'to help with the expenses of the dowry.' The phrase '财礼' refers to the dowry.
  • 陈笑曰 - Means 'Chen smiled and said.' The word '笑' means 'to smile.'
  • 遂厚遗其母 - Means 'then gave a generous gift to his mother.' The phrase '厚遗' means 'to give a generous gift.'
  • 携王归江南 - Means 'took Wang back to Jiangnan.' The phrase '携归' means 'to take back.'
  • 卒 - Means 'died.' This is a concise way to indicate death.
  • 皆得守其家业 - Means 'both were able to maintain their family estates.' The phrase '守其家业' means 'to maintain one's family estate.'
  • 人多所称述云 - Means 'people often spoke highly of them.' The phrase '称述' means 'to speak highly of.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 端朝文华富赡 - Describes the literary talent and richness of Wen Hua's writing.
  • 琼琼知其非久于白屋者 - Indicates that Qiongqiong recognized that Wen Hua was not destined to remain in poverty.
  • 遂倾心事之 - Means that Qiongqiong devoted herself wholeheartedly to Wen Hua.
  • 凡百费用，皆为办给 - Indicates that all expenses were provided for by Qiongqiong.
  • 时秋试高中，捷报之来，琼琼大出犒赏 - Describes the celebration when Wen Hua passed the autumn examination.
  • 及春闱省试，复中优等 - Indicates that Wen Hua again passed the spring examination with high honors.
  • 以策语过激，遂置下甲，注授南昌尉 - Explains that due to the radical nature of his policy proposals, Wen Hua was ranked lower and appointed as an official in Nanchang.
  • 琼琼恳曰 - Indicates that Qiongqiong pleaded with Wen Hua.
  • 妾风尘卑末，荷君不弃 - Means that Qiongqiong, a woman of lowly status, was grateful for Wen Hua's acceptance.
  • 今幸荣登仕版，行将云泥隔绝 - Indicates that Qiongqiong was now honored as an official but would soon be separated from him.
  • 忍使妾之一身，终沦弃乎 - Expresses Qiongqiong's fear of being abandoned.
  • 倘获脱此业缘，永执箕帚，受赐于君，诚不浅浅 - Means that if she could escape her current life and serve him as a wife, she would be deeply grateful.
  • 君内政虽严，妾自能小心承顺 - Indicates that Qiongqiong believed she could manage Wen Hua's strict household.
  • 且妾箱箧稍充，若与力图去籍，亦未为难 - Suggests that Qiongqiong had some savings and could help secure his release from his official duties.
  • 端朝曰 - Indicates that Wen Hua responded to Qiongqiong.
  • 去籍易耳 - Means that it would be easy to resign from his official duties.
  • 但内子非能容人者 - Indicates that Wen Hua's wife was not likely to accept Qiongqiong.
  • 设能相容，何待今日 - Suggests that if his wife could accept Qiongqiong, they would not be in this situation now.
  • 既汝中心诚恳，沮之则近无情，从之则虞有辱 - Explains that Wen Hua was torn between his wife's potential rejection and his own feelings for Qiongqiong.
  • 容先入数语探之 - Means that Wen Hua decided to speak to his wife first.
  • 如其不从，亦无策矣 - Indicates that if his wife refused, there would be no other options.
  • 因乘间谓其妻曰 - Means that Wen Hua took the opportunity to speak to his wife.
  • 我久居学舍，急于干禄，岂得待数年之阙 - Explains that Wen Hua had been living in the academy and was eager for official rank, and could not wait for a long period.
  • 近得一官，实出妓子马琼琼所赐 - Indicates that Wen Hua's recent appointment was due to Qiongqiong's support.

  • 好名耶，好才耶？曰，好色耳 - 好名 (hǎomíng) means 'to love fame,' 好才 (hǎocái) means 'to love talent,' 曰 (yuē) means 'said,' and 好色耳 (hǎosè ěr) means 'love of beauty.' This phrase poses a question and answers it with a statement about love of beauty.

  • 呜呼！若李师师者，可云有情，亦可云无赖者也 - 呜呼 (wū hū) is an expression of sorrow or lament, 若李师师者 (ruò lǐ shīshī zhě) means 'like Li Shishi,' 可云有情 (kě yún yǒu qíng) means 'can be said to have emotion,' 亦可云无赖者也 (yì kě yún wúlài zhě yě) means 'can also be said to be a scoundrel.'   • 市酒肴复游湖中 - Bought wine and food and continued their boat ride on the lake.
  • 倦寝 - To fall asleep due to exhaustion.
  • 净慈寺藕花深处 - Deep lotus flowers near Jingci Temple.
  • 相抱投入水中 - Embraced each other and jumped into the water.
  • 舟人惊救不及而死 - The boatman was too late to save them and they died.
  • 长桥月、短桥月 - Moonlit scenes at Long Bridge and Short Bridge, popular subjects in songs and poetry.
  • 妙年 - Young and handsome man.
  • 外方人也 - Person from outside the region.
  • 目击画舫纷纭 - Saw the colorful boats on the lake.
  • 夷犹之兴 - Desire to linger and enjoy.
  • 亭午 - Midday.
  • 莲舫渔艇 - Boats used for lotus picking and fishing.
  • 具杯馔入舟 - Prepared food and drink and entered the boat.
  • 曲尽欢而归 - Enjoyed themselves thoroughly and returned home.
  • 殆无虚日 - Almost every day.
  • 价反倍于他舟 - The price doubled compared to other boats.
  • 事载《名姬传》 - The story is recorded in *The Biography of Famous Courtesans*.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 淳熙 - Era name of Emperor Xiaozong of Song, indicating the historical period.
  • 行都 - Capital city of the Southern Song Dynasty, Hangzhou, known for its cultural and economic prosperity.
  • 角妓 - Courtesans who performed arts and entertained guests in the entertainment districts.
  • 恶姥 - Wicked or malicious old woman, often used to describe someone who interferes in relationships.
  • 净慈寺 - Famous Buddhist temple in Hangzhou, known for its scenic beauty and cultural significance.
  • 长桥月、短桥月 - Popular themes in Song Dynasty poetry and literature, often associated with romantic and tragic stories.
  • 名姬传 - Historical text that records the lives and stories of famous courtesans.

Proper Nouns

  • 荡子 - Young man who is dissolute or promiscuous.
  • 净慈寺 - Famous Buddhist temple in Hangzhou.
  • 长桥月、短桥月 - Popular themes in Song Dynasty poetry and literature.
  • 名姬传 - Historical text that records the lives and stories of famous courtesans.

[412]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 死后值钱者 - Literally 'things that are valuable after death,' a common phrase in classical texts to discuss posthumous value or legacy.
  • 惟杨太真袜、陶师儿舟 - Only the socks of Yang Tai-zhen and the boat of Tao Shi-er. These are specific items mentioned, likely referring to notable historical figures or artifacts.
  • 然袜以色贵，舟以情贵 - However, the socks are valued for their color, and the boat is valued for its emotion. This implies that the value lies in aesthetic or emotional qualities.
  • 约以赵氏，故不立许氏，使天下无出赵氏上者 - The promise was made to the Zhao family, so I did not establish Xu as empress, making sure no one would surpass the Zhao family.
  • 毋忧也 - Do not worry.
  • 凡掖庭中御幸生子者，辄死 - All those in the imperial harem who were favored by the emperor and gave birth to children were killed.
  • 又饮药伤堕者无数 - Moreover, countless women were harmed by poison to induce abortion.
  • 帝崩，竟无子 - When the emperor died, he left no heir.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杨太真袜 - Refers to the socks of Yang Tai-zhen, likely a historical figure or artifact of significance.
  • 陶师儿舟 - Refers to the boat of Tao Shi-er, another historical figure or artifact.
  • 汉成帝 - Emperor Cheng of Han, a ruler of the Western Han dynasty.
  • 赵后 - Zhao as the empress, indicating a specific historical event.
  • 昭仪 - A high-ranking concubine title.
  • 许美人 - Xu Mei-ren, a favored concubine.
  • 中黄门 - A middle yellow gate officer, a position in the imperial court.
  • 掖庭 - The imperial harem.
  • 中宫史 - A palace historian.

Proper Nouns

  • 陶师儿 - Tao Shi-er, a historical figure.
  • 汉成帝 - Emperor Cheng of Han.
  • 赵后 - Zhao as the empress.
  • 昭仪 - A high-ranking concubine title.

  • 中黄门 - A middle yellow gate officer.

[413]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 事孰大于继祠承祧者 - Rhetorical question meaning 'What matter is greater than continuing the ancestral sacrifices and inheriting the family line?'
  • 斩艾血胤以媚二淫 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to cut off the bloodline and sacrifice it to please the two debauched women.' '斩艾' is a metaphor for destroying the lineage, and '血胤' refers to the bloodline or descendants.
  • 其心死矣 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'their hearts are dead,' indicating a state of moral corruption or despair.
  • 假令收宫中美人子，卵而翼之，如刘皇后之于李宸妃，异日其子为君，犹未必仇赵氏也 - Complex sentence meaning 'Even if they had taken the sons of beautiful concubines from the palace, raised them like eggs under a wing, as Liu Empress did with Li Xianfei, in the future when their son became emperor, he might not necessarily hold a grudge against the Zhao family.'
  • 自绝其根，身名俱丧，不惟成帝痴，二淫者亦大痴矣 - Concluding statement meaning 'By cutting off their own roots, they lost both their lives and their reputations. Not only was Emperor Cheng foolish, but the two debauched women were also greatly foolish.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 继祠承祧 - Refers to the continuation of ancestral sacrifices and the inheritance of the family line, a crucial duty in traditional Chinese society.
  • 飞燕 - The nickname of Empress Zhao Feiyan, a concubine of Emperor Cheng of Han who rose to power.
  • 合德 - The nickname of Consort Zhao Hede, another influential concubine of Emperor Cheng.
  • 中宫 - Refers to the position of Empress.
  • 昭仪 - A high-ranking title for a concubine in the imperial harem.
  • 成帝 - Refers to Emperor Cheng of Han, the ruler during the time of Zhao Feiyan and Zhao Hede.
  • 刘皇后 - Refers to Empress Liu, the mother of Emperor Renzong of Song.
  • 李宸妃 - Refers to Consort Li, the mother of Emperor Renzong of Song.
  • 赵氏 - Refers to the Zhao family, likely a reference to the family of Zhao Feiyan and Zhao Hede.

Proper Nouns

  • 飞燕 - Empress Zhao Feiyan.
  • 合德 - Consort Zhao Hede.
  • 成帝 - Emperor Cheng of Han.
  • 刘皇后 - Empress Liu.
  • 李宸妃 - Consort Li.
  • 赵氏 - The Zhao family.

  • 殺圍 - Refers to another round of hunting; scholars interpret this as an ominous sign.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 北齊 - Refers to the Northern Qi dynasty, one of the Northern dynasties in Chinese history.
  • 後主 - Refers to the last ruler of a dynasty.
  • 大穆後 - Refers to Empress Dowager Mu, the mother of Emperor Gao Wei.
  • 平陽 - Refers to the city of Pingyang, which was captured by the Northern Zhou forces.
  • 三堆 - Refers to San Dui (Three Piles), a hunting ground.
  • 晉州 - Refers to Jizhou, a city in modern-day Shanxi.
  • 周師 - Refers to the Northern Zhou army.
  • 長安 - Refers to Chang'an, the capital of the Northern Zhou dynasty.
  • 周武帝 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Northern Zhou, the ruler of the Northern Zhou dynasty.
